title: Ad Placement on the Article Page
description:
This document provides detailed information about ad placement within the Article Page of the website. The following guidelines outline the placement and naming conventions for ads to ensure consistency and optimal user experience.

Ad Placement

1. Top of the Page

  • The first ad is loaded at the top of the page.

  • For the Article Page, the ad placement is denoted as:

    • desktop_ap_top for desktop users.

    • mobile_ap_top for mobile users.


2. In-Content Ads

  • In-content ads are displayed within the article content to enhance visibility and engagement.

  • Placement Details:

    1. The first in-content ad appears after the first paragraph.


    2. Subsequent ads appear after every third paragraph.


Important Consideration:

  • Headings are also counted as paragraphs when calculating ad placement.


For any further assistance create a ticket using help widget available in the dashboard or reach out to support@thepublive.com

title: Configuring the ads.txt File on the Dashboard
description:
This document outlines the steps required for publishers to configure and update the ads.txt file through the platform. Proper configuration of the ads.txt file ensures compliance with ad network requirements and helps maintain a secure and transparent advertising ecosystem.

Overview

The ads.txt file is now fully configurable by the publisher. This functionality enables publishers to easily manage the file without requiring additional technical support. The process can be completed through the platform's Configuration section.

Configuration Section Availability

  • The Ads.txt section is available to Owner and Admins users across all accounts.


Steps to Update the ads.txt File

Step 1: Login to Dashboard

Step 2: Locate and click on the Configuration option in the left menu bar


Step 3: Within the Configuration section, select the Miscellaneous (Misc) tab


Step 4: Click on the expand icon in the Ads.txt option


Step 5: Update the ads.txt file by:

  • Making the necessary changes to the content of the file
  • Saving your changes to apply them.


Key Features

  • Ease of Use: Publishers can directly modify the ads.txt file without relying on external support.

  • Centralized Management: All configurations related to the ads.txt file are accessible within the Miscellaneous section.

  • Compliance Assurance: Ensures adherence to industry standards by allowing timely updates to the ads.txt file.

Notes

  • Ensure that the ads.txt file content follows the guidelines provided by your ad network.

For any further assistance create a ticket using help widget available in the dashboard or reach out to support@thepublive.com

title: How to make any article as Featured Posts?
description:
This document is designed to help publishers to feature their posts, an excellent way to highlight important content on your website. Featured posts ensures they gain more visibility and remain prominently displayed at the top of your site.


Steps to make the post as featured:


Step 1: Log In and Navigate to the Published Section

  • Log in to the PubLive Dashboard.


  • Navigate to Post -> Click Published section to view all your published articles.




Step 2:Select and Feature an Article

  • Choose the articles you want to feature.
  • Click on the Set as Featured Post option.


Step 3: Confirm Featured Status

  • Once marked as featured, the selected article will:

    • Appear with a blue asterisk in the Published section.
    • Automatically move to the Featured section of the dashboard.


Step 4: Manage Featured Post Visibility

  • Use the drag-and-drop functionality to rearrange the order of featured articles.
  • The priority numbering is automatically assigned when the user rearranges the items.
  • Click Save after making changes to ensure the updated order reflects on the website.


Note:

  •  A maximum of 20 featured posts can be displayed in the Featured Posts section on the website. Ensure to prioritize your articles accordingly.
  • Each featured post has a Delete and Preview option visible in the Featured section.
  • Deleting an article from the Featured section will not delete the article itself. It will only remove the post from the featured list, keeping it available in the Published section.


Best Practices:

  • Prioritize High-Quality Content: Feature articles that are well-researched, engaging, and valuable to your audience. Highlighting subpar content can diminish the effectiveness of the feature.
  • Regularly Update Featured Posts: Keep the featured section dynamic by rotating articles frequently. Showcase seasonal or trending content to maintain audience interest.
  • Align with Business Goals: Select articles that align with your website’s objectives, such as driving sales, increasing awareness, or promoting specific campaigns.
  • Use Engaging Visuals: Ensure featured articles have eye-catching thumbnails and titles to immediately capture visitor attention.
  • Track Performance: Use analytics tools to monitor the traffic and engagement metrics of featured articles. Optimize your selection based on these insights.

Use Cases :

  • Product Launch Announcements: Showcase new product launches or updates to drive awareness and sales.
  • Trending Topics: Feature articles covering trending news, popular topics, or viral stories to boost traffic.
  • Evergreen Content: Promote timeless, high-value articles that continuously attract readership over time.
  • Event Promotions: Draw attention to upcoming events, webinars, or workshops by featuring related posts.
  • Content Series: Spotlight parts of a multi-article series, encouraging readers to explore related articles.

Benefits:

  • Enhanced Visibility: Featuring posts ensures important content remains prominently displayed, capturing the audience’s attention immediately upon visiting the website.
  • Increased Engagement: By highlighting compelling content, featured posts encourage users to click and interact more with the website.
  • Improved SEO Performance: Featuring frequently updated, high-quality articles can improve your website's search engine rankings by signaling relevance and freshness.
  • Audience Targeting: Allows publishers to direct readers to content that meets specific objectives, such as promotions or announcements.
  • Better Content Organization: Featured posts help create a structured and visually appealing layout, making it easier for readers to navigate.


All featured posts will appear at the top of your website in the order specified in the Featured section of the dashboard. This allows you to prioritize and control which articles are most visible to your audience.

title: What is Photo Gallery and How it works?
description:
The Photo Gallery feature enables users to showcase multiple images in a visually appealing layout, enhancing content presentation and engagement. It allows publishers to create immersive visual experiences, improving content visibility in search results and attracting more audience interaction.


How to Use the Photo Gallery

Step 1: Log in to Your Dashboard

  • Log in to the PubLive Dashboard page and enter your credentials.


Step 2: Create a New Photo Gallery

  • Go to Content Creation -> Photo Gallery, and start a new project.



Step 3: Creating Your First Gallery

  • Fill in the necessary information:
    • Title
    • English Title
    • Summary
    • Meta Description
    • Featured Image (option to hide if needed)



Step 4: Upload Images in Gallery Slides

  • Select an image from the media library and upload it into the slides.


  • Click the Upload Image button to upload images from your system to the media library.



  • Add image details such as caption, source, name, and alt text for better SEO and accessibility.


Step 5: Organize Your Gallery

  • Edit images anytime by clicking the Edit Icon.
  • Rearrange images by dragging and dropping them into your desired order.
  • Click the Delete Icon to remove an image from the slider.
  • Click the Preview Icon to open the image in a modal for better viewing.



Step 6: Set the post properties:
 
Step 7: Advanced Properties Explained:

  • Access Level: Use this field to specify if the articles/posts is for logged-in users or public access.
  • Custom Date: Use this field for backdating purposes.
  • Select Schema: Choose a schema for the article.
  • Canonical URL: Provide the canonical URL for this content.
  • Meta Title: The main title displayed in search engine results.
  • OG Title: The title used when your content is shared on social media.
  • OG Description: A brief summary shown when your content is shared on social media.
  • Twitter Title: The title specifically for Twitter shares.
  • Twitter Description: A brief description for Twitter shares.
  • Meta Keywords: These keywords provide information about a web page's content to search engines.
  • Meta News Keywords: Specifically used by Google News publishers to define keywords relevant to their articles.
  • Code Injection Head: Include custom code to be injected into the head section of your web pages.
  • Custom Style Injection: Apply custom CSS styles to your posts



Step 8: Preview & Publish

  • The content will only be updated, not republished on clicking Update button.


  • You can review the version history to restore any previous changes if needed.


  • You have the flexibility to schedule your post for publishing at your convenience.



Note: 

  • The Advanced Properties section is accessible exclusively to Admin & Owners to ensure security and prevent unauthorized modifications. Other users cannot view or edit this section.
  • Hide the featured image to showcase the photo gallery directly on the article page by enabling "Hide Featured Image" as shown below





Assisted “Fix it” Feature:

  • Monitor Warnings and keep track of your SEO score,following the recommendations for fixes.
  • The recommended SEO score should be (90+) for SEO purpose

 

As shown below, the visibility of the Photo gallery on the website



How Does This Feature Help Your Business?


  • Enhanced Visual Appeal: A photo gallery allows publishers to showcase their content in a visually appealing manner, attracting and retaining the attention of website visitors.

  • Improved User Engagement: High-quality images and an intuitive user interface encourage visitors to explore content further, increasing engagement and time spent on the website.

  • Effective Storytelling: A well-curated photo gallery can effectively tell a story or convey a message, capturing the audience's interest and facilitating better communication of ideas.

  • Increased Brand Visibility: By presenting content in an aesthetically pleasing manner, publishers can enhance their brand image and reputation, fostering a positive perception among visitors.

  • Optimized SEO: Image-rich content can improve search engine optimization (SEO) by attracting organic traffic through image search results. Additionally, optimizing image metadata can further enhance visibility in search engine rankings.

  • Social Media Promotion: Compelling images from the photo gallery can be easily shared on social media platforms, increasing brand exposure and driving traffic back to the website.

Best Practices :

  • Use High-Quality Images – Clear, high-resolution images enhance the user experience.
  • Maintain a Consistent Theme – Ensure images align with the article’s purpose or story.
  • Optimize Image Titles and Descriptions – Use descriptive and SEO-friendly text for better discoverability.
  • Ensure Mobile-Friendliness – Preview on multiple devices to maintain a seamless experience.
  • Limit the Number of Images – Avoid overloading the gallery; keep it concise and relevant.

Use Cases :

  • E-commerce – Showcase product collections with high-quality images.

  • Event Coverage – Highlight key moments from conferences, weddings, or exhibitions.

  • Travel & Tourism – Share travel experiences with stunning visuals.

  • Food & Recipes – Present step-by-step recipe visuals for better user engagement.

  • Real Estate & Architecture – Display property listings and architectural designs.

  • Fashion & Lifestyle – Feature style guides, fashion collections, or home decor ideas.

title: How to upload PDF in Media Library?
description:
The Dashboard now supports PDF uploads into the Media Library. Below are the steps for uploading and using PDF files effectively.


Method 1: Uploading a PDF to the Media Library and Utilizing Its Link:


Step 1 Login to your Dashboard

  • Login to the Publive Dashboard using by entering the credentials.


Step 2 Accessing the Media Library:

  • Click on Media Library in the left menu bar.


Step 3 Selecting the File Library:

  • In the Media Library, navigate to the Files section.


Step 4 Uploading the PDF File:

  • Click the Upload File button.


  • Select the PDF file from your local folder.


  • By default, the file name will be displayed after uploading. You may rename it if needed then click the Upload button.
  • Once uploaded, the file will be available in the File Library.

Note: Maximum allowed size of PDF is 50 MB.


Step 5 How to Get the PDF Link?

  • Navigate to the File Library and select the uploaded PDF file and Click Copy to Clipboard to copy the PDF link.


  • You can use the copied PDF link as per your requirement.
  • You can click on View File to preview the uploaded PDF file.


  • Below is Preview of PDF (it opens in a new tab)


Method 2: Uploading a PDF via the Content Editor


Step 1 Accessing the Media Library

  • In Content Editor, click on the Media Library from the quick help tool.


  • This will open the Media Library window.


Step 2 Uploading the PDF File:

  • Follow the instructions mentioned above in Step-3 of Method 1.


Utilizing the PDF in Different Ways:


Method 1: Copy the PDF link from the File Library and paste it directly into the editor.

  • After clicking "Copy to Clipboard", paste the link (ctrl+v) directly in the editor as shown below


Method 2: Insert the PDF link as a hyperlink using the Link Tool in the editor.

  • After clicking "Copy to Clipboard", click the link tool as shown below


  • A pop-up windown will open. Now paste the copied link of the PDF in the URL field and add the text in Text to display  -> Click Save


  • Below is the view of Hyperlink


Method 3: Insert the PDF directly into the content using the Insert Media option.

  • Navigate to the File Library and select the uploaded PDF file and Click Insert to insert the PDF.


  • Below is the preview of the PDF added in the Editor

title: How does Smart Embeds work in Editor?
description:
Smart Embed feature refers to the ability to display content from one website or application within another, allowing for a more visually appealing and interactive experience for users. It can be easily done by copy-paste the social posts link into the Editor of the PubLive Dashboard. When you paste the link of a social media post, the Smart embed automatically generates a preview of the webpage, showing its title, description, and image. This gives viewers a glimpse of the content before they decide to click on the link. 


Smart embeds can also display other types of media, such as videos, audio players, interactive maps, slideshows, and more. They enhance the user experience by providing a more dynamic and engaging way to interact with content without leaving the website.


To get started, copy the social post link/URL and paste it into the editor


Editor View

 

Website view


Similarly, you can use smart embedding for frequently used popular social platforms

  • Youtube
  • Facebook
  • Twitter
  • Instagram
  • Spotify


Other platforms include,

  • Slideshare
  • SoundCloud
  • Reddit
  • Tiktok
  • Pinterest
  • Tumblr


Note: You can also use social embed code but direct paste of social embed code in the editor is deprecated for security reasons. You can add social embed code using Insert/Edit Media option. 

Go to Insert -> Media -> Insert/Edit Media modal pops-up as shown below  



How does this feature help Digital Publishers?



Increased Engagement: Smart embeds make content more visually appealing and interactive. When users encounter visually enticing previews, such as images, videos, or interactive elements, they are more likely to engage with the content and click on the link to view the full content. This increased engagement can lead to higher website traffic and more views for the publisher's content.


Improved User Experience: By providing additional context and information through rich previews, publishers can improve the overall user experience. Readers can quickly grasp what the content is about and decide whether they want to explore it further. This saves users' time and makes them more likely to consume the content.


Better Content Promotion: When publishers share their content on various platforms with smart embeds, it becomes easier for others to share that content with their audiences. The appealing previews make it more likely for people to share the content, leading to organic promotion and wider exposure.


Cross-Platform Compatibility: Smart embeds work across different devices and platforms, ensuring that the content is accessible and displayed optimally on various devices, including desktops, smartphones, and tablets.


SEO Benefits: Some smart embeds utilize metadata markup, like Twitter Cards, which can enhance search engine optimization (SEO). These metadata tags provide search engines with valuable information about the content, potentially improving its search rankings and visibility.


Media Support: Smart embeds can display various types of media, such as images, videos, audio players, maps, and slideshows. This versatility allows publishers to present their content in engaging ways that cater to different preferences.

Overall,  Smart embeds contribute to a more user-friendly and visually appealing online experience, making it easier for people to discover and engage with content from various sources.

title: How to Make Template Pages More Impactful with Custom Content?
description:
The PubLive Dashboard allows you to create template pages for various purposes, such as announcements, newsletters, FAQs, events, and more. This guide will walk you through the process of creating a custom page using the HTML Source Code Editor, available under View > Source Code.
Steps to Create a Template Page:
Step 1: Log in to the PubLive Dashboard

  • Open your browser and go to the PubLive Dashboard.
  • Enter your login credentials and sign in to your account.



Step 2: Navigate to the Custom Content Section

  • Navigate to Posts -> Custom Content -> Click on the  “+” -> Template page.



  • This will open a new editor where you can build your page.


Step 3: Steps to Open the HTML Source Code Editor

  • In the new Template Page Editor, navigate to the Content Section -> View.
  • Select Source Code to open the HTML Source Code Editor modal.



Below is the sample custom FAQ page built using the HTML source code editor in the PubLive dashboard.

Step 4: Paste Your Custom HTML Code

  • Prepare the HTML code for your custom page.



  • Paste it into the Source Code Editor.
  • Click Save to apply your changes.


Step 5: Preview Your Template Page

  • After saving, the visual representation of your page will update in the editor.
  • Review the design to ensure all elements appear correctly.


Step 6: Advanced Properties Explained:

  • Access Level: Use this field to specify if the articles/posts is for logged-in users or public access.
  • Custom Date: Use this field for backdating purposes.
  • Select Schema: Choose a schema for the article.
  • Canonical URL: Provide the canonical URL for this content.
  • Meta Title: The main title displayed in search engine results.
  • OG Title: The title used when your content is shared on social media.
  • OG Description: A brief summary shown when your content is shared on social media.
  • Twitter Title: The title specifically for Twitter shares.
  • Twitter Description: A brief description for Twitter shares.
  • Meta Keywords: These keywords provide information about a web page's content to search engines.
  • Meta News Keywords: Specifically used by Google News publishers to define keywords relevant to their articles.
  • Code Injection Head: Include custom code to be injected into the head section of your web pages.
  • Custom Style Injection: Apply custom CSS styles to your posts.


Note: 

  • The Advanced Properties section is accessible exclusively to Admin & Owners to ensure security and prevent unauthorized modifications. Other users cannot view or edit this section.
  • Monitor Warnings and keep track of your SEO score,following the recommendations for fixes.
    The recommended SEO score should be (90+) for SEO purpose.


Step 7: Publish and Use the Template Page

  • Once satisfied with the design, click Publish.
  • Utilize it for your desired purpose, such as:
  • Static pages
  • Announcements
  • Newsletters
  • Event details
  • FAQ sections         
  • You can reuse or modify the page for future needs.


    To make the custom page easily accessible, you can:

  • Add it to the Footer – Link it under sections like "Resources" or "Support."
  • Include it in the Navbar – Display it in the main navigation for quick access.
  • Share the Link – Use it in emails, social media, or internal communications.

Best Practices :

  • Use Clean and Structured HTML: Well-formatted code improves readability and maintenance.
  • Separate Styling and Scripts: Keep CSS and JavaScript in the Advanced section for better organization.
  • Optimize for Performance: Use compressed images and minimize unnecessary scripts.
  • Cross-Browser Compatibility: Test on multiple browsers to ensure a consistent experience.
  • Enhance Accessibility: Use alt tags for images and ARIA attributes for better usability.


Benefits:

  • Flexibility: Customize pages for different purposes (FAQs, newsletters, announcements, etc.).
  • Brand Consistency: Align designs with your brand’s identity.
  • Better User Engagement: Well-structured pages improve user experience.

  • Seamless Integration: Works smoothly with PubLive's existing features.

title: Permalink Editing
description:
We are thrilled to announce a new feature that will enable the publishers to update the link of the published article: Permalink Editing Feature. Permalinks are the permanent URLs that point to a specific article, and allowing edits to them after publication can help in various scenarios, such as fixing typos, improving SEO, or updating outdated information without breaking existing links. 

Note: The Permalink Editing feature in the dashboard is accessible to Owners and Admin only

To get started, go to the published section. You can find the Edit Permalink option in the extended menu as shown below.  

 

Click the Edit Permalink for the article to change the URL. It will open a modal where you can provide the new URL as shown below

  • Current Permalink - Current URL of the article
  • Enter Permalink - Where you need to provide the new URL/link of the article. If there is any issue in the format, give a warning in red.   

Once done and if the provided link has no issue in its format then the Update Permalink button will be enabled. Now, click the button to update

Once the Update Permalink button is clicked, a notification will be displayed as shown below. Now the article will have the updated link.

Note: The old link of an article will be redirected to the new link. If anyone visits the old link, will be redirected automatically to the new URL without breaking or causing errors.

 These Permalinks will also be visible below the Permalink Field for the article in the dashboard under edit mode as shown below  

 

 

How does this feature help Digital Publishers?

Correction of Errors: The ability to edit permalinks allows authors and editors to correct typos, grammatical errors, or other mistakes in URLs without having to delete and recreate the entire article or content item.

Improved SEO: Permalink editing can be valuable for search engine optimization (SEO). If you discover that a permalink could be better optimized for search engines, you can make adjustments without losing the SEO benefits already gained from the existing content.

Update Outdated Information: Over time, content may become outdated. With permalink editing, you can update the content and adjust the permalink to reflect the changes while maintaining the integrity of existing links.

Content Reorganization: If you want to reorganize your content structure, permalink editing allows you to change the URL structure to better fit your requirements without breaking existing links.

Enhanced User Experience: Permalink editing helps maintain a positive user experience by ensuring that users always land on the correct and up-to-date content, even if they access it through older links or bookmarks.

Preventing Broken Links: Instead of creating a new article with a corrected URL and potentially leaving behind broken links to the old one, you can edit the permalink and set up redirects to ensure a seamless transition for users and search engines.

Flexibility in Content Creation: It provides content creators with more flexibility in naming articles or pages, allowing for creativity and optimization without being constrained by initially chosen URLs.

Content Maintenance: As your content evolves, you can use permalink editing to keep your URLs in sync with the content, reducing confusion for both users and content creators.

Implementation of a permalink editing feature for published articles can enhance the flexibility and maintenance of your website's content while preserving a positive user experience. However, it should be used judiciously and with careful consideration of its potential impacts on SEO and user navigation.

title: User Generated Content (UGC)
description:
User-generated content (UGC) for digital publishers enables content created by the audience or users of a digital platform, rather than being exclusively generated by the publisher's editorial team. This form of content has become increasingly integral to the digital landscape, offering a range of benefits to publishers seeking to enhance engagement and expand their online presence.


Note: This feature is available for publishers under Advanced, or Premium Tiers


To get started with UGC, go to the Forms section in the dashboard.


As shown below, provided with two tabs 


1. Forms - List of forms created

2. Submission - All submissions against the form from the readers will be available in this tab 



Key Note: This Form section and submissions are visible only to Owners and Admins


Click 'Add New Form' -> Select 'Share your story'   


Note: Currently, Share your story is the format available and Custom Form is coming soon! 




Provide the Form Name and click Save




Fill the following information for the form to be created


  • Form Name - Which you have provided above
  • Title - Title of the form which is displayed in this form page on website
  • Title in English - As the name conveys, title in English to be provided. If your title is provided in non-english language, then this field should be provided in English. As it will be the url of the form
  • Description - Content for the form page 
  • Featured Image - Banner image of the form 


Note: Fields with * are mandatory 


Once all information are provided click Save




Readers' field preview are available below



Once saved, Form will be successfully created and will be available in Forms tab as shown below. User can view form, view submissions, edit and delete. 



Note: Can add new form anytime by clicking Add New Form on the top-right as shown below. Next to it, is search form by name available


View Form option in the dashboard will take to corresponding form page in website. Ensure to login to view the form being created

 



Website | Form Page


Readers can use this page to submit their stories




Below is the same on how the readers can submit their story to you



Once they click Send, below is the confirmation message will be shown to the readers upon submission

 



Dashboard | Forms section


To view the submissions click View Submission (Option to view the submission for this specific form) or go to Submission tab (list of submissions for all the forms) 



On click of View Submission, list of submission for a specific form is available. Following are the details


  • Form name 
  • Email of the reader who submitted the form 
  • Form Type 
  • Submitted Date 
  • Action - View and Unseen/Seen       



Under Action -> View will take to reader submission post where you can 

  • Send to draft - moves the posts to draft section of the dashboard
  • Status - status of the form
  • Notes - You can add private notes as shown below. Once you submit, it will be available for that posts
  • Timeline - Any action performed will be recorded in this timeline with required details




On click of "Send to Draft", the posts will be saved to draft section. You can Edit Story/View Story as shown below

  



 


You can edit the story after required formatting and publish once done 




Once published, it will be available in the published section as shown below






Note: Use Team section to first add the contributor. Then, the name is going to be available in the credit dropdown in create/edit mode, you can use this field to give credit to the reader whoever submitted the article 


 


This way you can create and manage forms and reader submissions



How it benefits the digital publishers


1. Authenticity and Trust UGC is prized for its authenticity. In a digital era saturated with branded content, user-generated material provides a refreshing authenticity that resonates with audiences. The trust users place in content generated by their peers contributes to a more credible and genuine online environment.


2. Diverse Perspectives: Embracing UGC enables digital publishers to tap into a wealth of diverse perspectives, experiences, and voices. This diversity enriches the content landscape, making it more inclusive and reflective of the varied interests and backgrounds of the audience.


3. Enhanced Engagement: UGC fosters higher levels of engagement as it invites active participation from the audience. Digital platforms that encourage users to contribute content, share experiences, and participate in discussions benefit from increased user interaction, longer time spent on the platform, and higher click-through rates.


4. Cost-Effective Content Creation: Digital publishers often face the challenge of producing a constant stream of fresh and engaging content. UGC provides a cost-effective solution as users contribute content voluntarily, reducing the burden on the publisher's resources while maintaining a steady flow of material.


5. Community Building: UGC plays a pivotal role in building a sense of community around a digital platform. When users feel their contributions are valued, it fosters a sense of belonging and encourages sustained participation, creating a vibrant and loyal community.


7. SEO Benefits: Search engines favor dynamic and regularly updated content. UGC, when appropriately managed and curated, contributes to a continuously evolving digital landscape, improving search engine rankings and driving organic traffic to the publisher's website.


Overall, Incorporating user-generated content into digital publishing strategies enhances authenticity, diversifies content offerings, and fosters a more engaged and loyal audience. Digital publishers can harness the power of UGC to build vibrant online communities, improve search visibility, and create a more dynamic and responsive digital presence.

Following are the details      Form name   Email of the reader who submitted the form   Form Type   Submitted Date   Action - View and Unseen/Seen                  Under Action -> View will take to reader submission post where you can    Send to draft - moves the posts to draft section of the dashboard  Status - status of the form  Notes - You can add private notes as shown below. Once you submit, it will be available for that posts  Timeline - Any action performed will be recorded in this timeline with required details              On click of "Send to Draft", the posts will be saved to draft section. You can Edit Story/View Story as shown below                    You can edit the story after required formatting and publish once done              Once published, it will be available in the published section as shown below                   Note: Use Team section to first add the contributor. Then, the name is going to be available in the credit dropdown in create/edit mode, you can use this field to give credit to the reader whoever submitted the article            This way you can create and manage forms and reader submissions        How it benefits the digital publishers     1. Authenticity and Trust UGC is prized for its authenticity. In a digital era saturated with branded content, user-generated material provides a refreshing authenticity that resonates with audiences. The trust users place in content generated by their peers contributes to a more credible and genuine online environment.     2. Diverse Perspectives: Embracing UGC enables digital publishers to tap into a wealth of diverse perspectives, experiences, and voices. This diversity enriches the content landscape, making it more inclusive and reflective of the varied interests and backgrounds of the audience.     3. Enhanced Engagement: UGC fosters higher levels of engagement as it invites active participation from the audience. Digital platforms that encourage users to contribute content, share experiences, and participate in discussions benefit from increased user interaction, longer time spent on the platform, and higher click-through rates.     4. Cost-Effective Content Creation: Digital publishers often face the challenge of producing a constant stream of fresh and engaging content. UGC provides a cost-effective solution as users contribute content voluntarily, reducing the burden on the publisher's resources while maintaining a steady flow of material.     5. Community Building: UGC plays a pivotal role in building a sense of community around a digital platform. When users feel their contributions are valued, it fosters a sense of belonging and encourages sustained participation, creating a vibrant and loyal community.     7. SEO Benefits: Search engines favor dynamic and regularly updated content. UGC, when appropriately managed and curated, contributes to a continuously evolving digital landscape, improving search engine rankings and driving organic traffic to the publisher's website.     Overall, Incorporating user-generated content into digital publishing strategies enhances authenticity, diversifies content offerings, and fosters a more engaged and loyal audience. Digital publishers can harness the power of UGC to build vibrant online communities, improve search visibility, and create a more dynamic and responsive digital presence.

title: How AI Web Story works?

title: Introducing the Enriched and Advanced UI for Web Stories!
description:
We are thrilled to announce a significant upgrade to our platform – the Enriched and Advanced UI for Web Stories! This enhancement revolutionizes your storytelling experience by seamlessly integrating powerful SEO recommendations and advanced properties into our platform, alongside a sleek and intuitive user interface.

What’s New:

  • Sleek Design: Enjoy a modern and streamlined user interface that enhances your workflow and makes creating web stories a joy.
  • Enhanced Functionality: Navigate through features seamlessly and effortlessly, thanks to improved usability and intuitive controls.
  • SEO Recommendations: Access actionable insights and recommendations to optimize your web stories for search engines, ensuring maximum visibility and engagement.
  • Advanced Properties: Customize essential elements such as OG title, description, meta title, Twitter meta title, and description, empowering you to fine-tune your story's appearance across various platforms.
  • Custom Style Ingestion: Effortlessly apply custom styles to maintain brand consistency and enhance the visual appeal of your web stories, all within our user-friendly interface.
  • Assisted Fix it:  Streamline the implementation of SEO recommendations with our new Assisted "Fix it" feature. This tool guides you directly to the specific modules and fields needing refinements, saving you time and effort by eliminating the guesswork. Enjoy efficient and accurate updates with ease.

Benefits for You:

With the Enriched and Advanced UI for Web Stories, you can take your storytelling to the next level. From optimizing for search engines to crafting visually stunning narratives, our platform provides you with the tools and guidance you need to captivate your audience and drive results.

Get Started:

To experience the Enriched and Advanced UI for Web Stories, log in to your account and explore the updated interface. Whether you're a seasoned storyteller or just getting started, our comprehensive documentation and tutorials are here to support you every step of the way.

Creating a Web Story

 A Step-by-Step Guide:

Step 1: Go to Create -> Web Story format 

 

Step 2: Provide the required details for the article.

Step 3: Upload custom thumbnails for both dimensions (optional).

By default, the cover image is considered a thumbnail, and the aspect ratios are:

  • 9:16 ratio | Used in Web Stories Section 

  • 16:9 ratio | Used in Home Page Category Section 

Step 4:  Upload the slides image, choose a template, set slide properties, and settings

Fill in the details for slide-wise settings, title, description, CTA text (Text of the CTA button), CTA link (hyperlink of the CTA button) 


Advanced Properties Explained:


  • Custom Date: Use this field for backdating purposes.
  • Meta Title: The main title displayed in search engine results.
  • OG Title: The title used when your content is shared on social media.
  • OG Description: A brief summary shown when your content is shared on social media.
  • Twitter Title: The title specifically for Twitter shares.
  • Twitter Description: A brief description for Twitter shares.
  • Custom Style Injection: Apply custom CSS styles to your web stories.

Choosing Template:


  • Template 1: Image in Portrait view with title and description overlayed at bottom
  • Template 2: Image in landscape view with title and description highlighted at center


Step 5: Monitor warnings and keep track of your SEO score, following the recommendations for fixes


Assisted “Fix it” Feature:

Introducing the Assisted "Fix it" feature, designed to streamline the implementation of SEO recommendations and suggestions. This feature guides you directly to the specific module and fields where refinements are needed, saving you time and effort. Instead of searching for what and where to fix, Assisted "Fix it" takes you straight to the required sections, ensuring efficient and accurate updates.


Step 6: Set the post properties


Step 7: Preview and publish your web story

 Template 1:


Template 2: 

 

How it benefits the digital publishers:

Creating web stories offers digital publishers a range of benefits that can significantly enhance their online presence and engagement metrics:

  1. Enhanced User Engagement: Web stories provide a visually appealing and immersive experience for users, leading to increased engagement with your content.

  2. Improved Visibility: Web stories are prominently featured in search engine results, especially on mobile devices, increasing the likelihood of attracting organic traffic to your website.

  3. Higher Click-Through Rates (CTR): The interactive and visually rich nature of web stories tends to result in higher CTRs compared to traditional static content formats.

  4. Mobile-Friendly Content: With the majority of internet users accessing content via mobile devices, web stories are optimized for mobile viewing, ensuring a seamless and enjoyable experience for mobile users.

  5. SEO Benefits: Web stories can contribute to your overall SEO strategy by providing additional opportunities to rank in search engine results pages (SERPs), particularly in the "Stories" carousel.

  6. Social Media Shareability: Web stories are highly shareable across social media platforms, allowing publishers to reach a wider audience and drive traffic back to their websites.

We can’t wait to see the incredible stories you’ll create with our new Enriched and Advanced UI for Web Stories!

To experience the Enriched and Advanced UI Article creation workflow, log in to the dashboard.


Go to Post -> Create -> Select Article Format





The new enhanced UI is much more organized and lets you manage the article creation flow more effectively.

Fill in the essential post details:

  • Title
  • English Title
  • Summary
  • Meta Description
  • Featured Image



Step 2: Content


You can provide your paragraphs, images, embeds, tables etc in this content section 


 



Step 3: Post Properties


Provide the following details:

  • Primary Category
  • Additional Category
  • Tags
  • Credits
  • Focus Keyphrase



Note: You can also click "Manage Post Properties" located at the top-right, which will guide you to the corresponding section. 



Step 4: SEO Warnings/Suggestions


Monitor warnings and keep track of your SEO score, following the recommendations for fixes



Assisted “Fix it” Feature:


Introducing the Assisted "Fix it" feature, designed to streamline the implementation of SEO recommendations and suggestions. This feature guides you directly to the specific module and fields where refinements are needed, saving you time and effort. Instead of searching for what and where to fix, Assisted "Fix it" takes you straight to the required sections, ensuring efficient and accurate updates.

  


Step 5: Advanced Post Properties


 

  • Access Level: Use this field to specify if the articles/posts is for logged-in users or public access.
  • Custom Date: Use this field for backdating purposes.
  • Select Schema: Choose a schema for the article.
  • Canonical URL: Provide the canonical URL for this content.
  • Meta Title: The main title displayed in search engine results.
  • OG Title: The title used when your content is shared on social media.
  • OG Description: A brief summary shown when your content is shared on social media.
  • Twitter Title: The title specifically for Twitter shares.
  • Twitter Description: A brief description for Twitter shares.
  • Code Injection Head: Include custom code to be injected into the head section of your web pages.
  • Custom Style Injection: Apply custom CSS styles to your posts



Also, for further assistance, provided with tooltips across the fields in the editor that you can refer to



Step 6: Preview and Publish the article


Finally, you can preview and publish the article. Hooray! 


To know more about our other new features, visit: 

  • Transforming Content Creation with Our AI Copilot
  • Introducing the Enriched and Advanced UI for Web Stories

title: Why should you not create tags on the go?
description:
Before we tell you why you shouldn't create tags on the go, let us acknowledge the importance of tags. Tags are crucial for organizing and categorizing content. Your readers can easily explore all posts in their interested tags.


Tags are good for you but the problem begins when you don't manage your tags. In fact, if you create too many posts in short intervals, you might be tempted to create a tag everytime you don't find the tag you are looking for!


The problems with too many tags are the following:

  1. Thin Content: However, since the newly created tags might not have a lot of content associated, they become "thin content". Further, Google may penalize sites with a high number of thin content pages, impacting your site's authority and ranking.

  2. Duplicate Content: Redundant tags can lead to duplicate content issues since you might have similar tags associated with different pieces of content of the same topic.


Neither does Google like thin content nor does it like duplicate content.


As such, we recommend the following best practices

  1. Predefined Tag List

Identify the most important keywords for your brand and create tags for the different types of content you'd create for those keywords. Try to stick to these tags to avoid tag redundancy.


  1. Regular Tag Audit

Conduct regular tag audits to find duplicate tags and merge similar ones. You can also delete unused tags to avoid thin content. This will help you keep a clean and efficient content management system.


  1. Training

Train your content creators on the importance of tag management. Share the best practices with them to ensure your website follows best practices.

title: Introducing Log Activity Feature to manage all your logs and activities
description:
Overview


The Log Activity feature, located in the Configuration section under "Log Activity," is essential for maintaining a comprehensive record of various activities within your platform. This feature is exclusively accessible to Owners and Admins, ensuring only authorized personnel can manage and review these logs.


Accessing Log Activity


To access the Log Activity feature:

  1. Navigate to the Configuration section.
  2. Select Log Activity from the menu.



Adding a New Activity Log 


Owners and Admins can add new activity logs by following these steps:

  1. Click the Add Activity button.
  2. Fill in the Description field with a detailed account of the activity.
  3. Add relevant Tags to categorize and easily search for the log later.
  4. Specify the Time of the activity. This can include past times, allowing for backdated logging of activities.



Added logs will be recorded in the Log Activity as shown below.


Search & Filter for Tags


Users can search by Tags in the search field to find relevants log activities. 


 

Additionally, can filter the logs by custom date range





Benefits of Log Activity

Purpose

The Log Activity feature serves multiple purposes:

  • Record-Keeping: Maintain a detailed history of significant activities and changes within the platform.
  • Accountability: Ensure all actions taken by the members are documented by Owners and Admins for future reference.
  • Transparency: Provide a clear and accessible trail of activities that can be reviewed as needed.


Benefits for Publishers

  1. Enhanced Oversight:

    • Publishers can maintain a clear and organized record of all significant activities, ensuring nothing is overlooked.
    • Helps in auditing and compliance by providing a transparent log of actions.

  2. Improved Accountability:

    • By logging activities, Owners and Admins can be held accountable.
    • Promotes responsible management practices within the organization.

  3. Efficient Troubleshooting:

    • Logs can be referred to when troubleshooting issues, helping to quickly identify and address the root causes of problems.
    • Provides a historical context that can aid in understanding the sequence of events leading up to an issue.

  4. Data-Driven Decisions:

    • Detailed logs provide valuable insights into platform usage and management activities, supporting data-driven decision-making.
    • Enables the identification of patterns and trends that can inform strategic planning.

Conclusion

The Log Activity feature is a powerful tool for Owners and Admins, enabling meticulous record-keeping, enhanced accountability, and improved oversight. By providing a detailed account of activities, this feature supports transparent and responsible management practices, ultimately benefiting the entire publishing operation.

title: Adding Fact Check Schema to your article?
description:
Approach 1

  1. Visit Google fact Check Markup tool:
    https://toolbox.google.com/factcheck/markuptool?authuser=2&pageId=none
  2. Enter Article URL for which you want to do Fact Check Markup:
  3. Complete the Steps on page, to generate markup code to be added on the article page:
    Here is an example of filled details for a fact check schema:
  4. Once you are done, you can also preview added markup: "View JSON-LD"

Approach 2

  1. Directly fill the fields for json-ld markup, here's a sample filled Fact Check json-ld markup:

  2. <script type="application/ld+json">
    {
      "@context": "https://schema.org",
      "@type": "ClaimReview",
      "url": "https://example.com/news/science/worldisflat.html",
      "claimReviewed": "The world is flat",
      "itemReviewed": {
        "@type": "Claim",
        "author": {
          "@type": "Organization",
          "name": "Square World Society",
          "sameAs": "https://example.flatworlders.com/we-know-that-the-world-is-flat"
        },
        "datePublished": "2024-06-20",
        "appearance": {
          "@type": "OpinionNewsArticle",
          "url": "https://example.com/news/a122121",
          "headline": "Square Earth - Flat earthers for the Internet age",
          "datePublished": "2024-06-22",
          "author": {
            "@type": "Person",
            "name": "T. Tellar"
          },
          "image": "https://example.com/photos/1x1/photo.jpg",
          "publisher": {
            "@type": "Organization",
            "name": "Skeptical News",
            "logo": {
              "@type": "ImageObject",
              "url": "https://example.com/logo.jpg"
            }
          }
        }
      },
      "author": {
        "@type": "Organization",
        "name": "Example.com science watch"
      },
      "reviewRating": {
        "@type": "Rating",
        "ratingValue": 1,
        "bestRating": 5,
        "worstRating": 1,
        "alternateName": "False"
      }
    }
    </script>

    For guidance on what to fill in the above markup fields, refer to google documentation of this markup:
    https://developers.google.com/search/docs/appearance/structured-data/factcheck

  3. Once you are done creating the above json-ld code, add this here in Publive Editor (in Advanced Properties toggle on the right) to have it as part of your article page code:

  4. Publish / Update the article once above is done.

title: Introducing the Enriched and Advanced UI for Web Stories!
description:
We are thrilled to announce a significant upgrade to our platform – the Enriched and Advanced UI for Web Stories! This enhancement revolutionizes your storytelling experience by seamlessly integrating powerful SEO recommendations and advanced properties into our platform, alongside a sleek and intuitive user interface.

What’s New:

  • Sleek Design: Enjoy a modern and streamlined user interface that enhances your workflow and makes creating web stories a joy.

  • Enhanced Functionality: Navigate through features seamlessly and effortlessly, thanks to improved usability and intuitive controls.

  • SEO Recommendations: Access actionable insights and recommendations to optimize your web stories for search engines, ensuring maximum visibility and engagement.

  • Advanced Properties: Customize essential elements such as OG title, description, meta title, Twitter meta title, and description, empowering you to fine-tune your story's appearance across various platforms.

  • Custom Style Ingestion: Effortlessly apply custom styles to maintain brand consistency and enhance the visual appeal of your web stories, all within our user-friendly interface.

  • Assisted Fix it:  Streamline the implementation of SEO recommendations with our new Assisted "Fix it" feature. This tool guides you directly to the specific modules and fields needing refinements, saving you time and effort by eliminating the guesswork. Enjoy efficient and accurate updates with ease.

Where to find this feature: 

  • This feature can be found in the Dashboard


Get Started:

To experience the Enriched and Advanced UI for Web Stories, log in to your account and explore the updated interface. Whether you're a seasoned storyteller or just getting started, our comprehensive documentation and tutorials are here to support you every step of the way.

Step 1: Login to Dashboard


Step 2: Creating Web Stories via 2 Methods 
Method 1: By using Side Navigation Menu
Method 2: By using Quick Create Menu



Step 3: Provide the required details for the article.



Step 4: Upload custom thumbnails for both dimensions (optional).

By default, the cover image is considered a thumbnail, and the aspect ratios are:

  • 9:16 ratio | Used in Web Stories Section 


  • 16:9 ratio | Used in Web Stories Section



Step 5: Upload the Images required for the web story

*Note: 

  • While uploading images to the Media Library, you can provide a caption and source, which will automatically reflect in your web story.
  • For existing images, you can edit their details to add a caption and source, which will then reflect accordingly.


Upload Images via Media Library



Edit image details via Media Library


Step 6: Upload the slides image, choose a template, set slide properties, and settings, Fill in the details for slide-wise settings, title, description, CTA text (Text of the CTA button), CTA link (hyperlink of the CTA button)

Note: 

  • While uploading images in the Media Library, if you add a source and caption, they will automatically reflect in the slides based on the selected templates.
  • The Title field should have a maximum character limit of 70 and can be set once on the first slide.
  • Description field should have a maximum character limit of 140
  • Number of slides recommended here - 8 slides


Advanced Properties Explained:

  • Custom Date: Use this field for backdating purposes.
  • Meta Title: The main title displayed in search engine results.
  • OG Title: The title used when your content is shared on social media.
  • OG Description: A brief summary shown when your content is shared on social media.
  • Twitter Title: The title specifically for Twitter shares.
  • Twitter Description: A brief description for Twitter shares.
  • Custom Style Injection: Apply custom CSS styles to your web stories.


Choosing Template:

  • Template 1: Image in Portrait view with title and description overlayed at bottom
  • Template 2: Image in landscape view with title and description highlighted at center
  • Template 3: Image in Portrait view with title, description, credits and published time and date

Step 7: Monitor warnings and keep track of your SEO score, following the recommendations for fixes


Assisted “Fix it” Feature:

Introducing the Assisted "Fix it" feature, designed to streamline the implementation of SEO recommendations and suggestions. This feature guides you directly to the specific module and fields where refinements are needed, saving you time and effort. Instead of searching for what and where to fix, Assisted "Fix it" takes you straight to the required sections, ensuring efficient and accurate updates.



Step 8: Set the post properties


Step 9: Preview and Publish your web story

Template 1:


Template 2:


Template 3:


How it benefits the digital publishers:

Creating web stories offers digital publishers a range of benefits that can significantly enhance their online presence and engagement metrics:

  1. Enhanced User Engagement: Web stories provide a visually appealing and immersive experience for users, leading to increased engagement with your content.

  2. Improved Visibility: Web stories are prominently featured in search engine results, especially on mobile devices, increasing the likelihood of attracting organic traffic to your website.

  3. Higher Click-Through Rates (CTR): The interactive and visually rich nature of web stories tends to result in higher CTRs compared to traditional static content formats.

  4. Mobile-Friendly Content: With the majority of internet users accessing content via mobile devices, web stories are optimized for mobile viewing, ensuring a seamless and enjoyable experience for mobile users.

  5. SEO Benefits: Web stories can contribute to your overall SEO strategy by providing additional opportunities to rank in search engine results pages (SERPs), particularly in the "Stories" carousel.

  6. Social Media Shareability: Web stories are highly shareable across social media platforms, allowing publishers to reach a wider audience and drive traffic back to their websites.

We can’t wait to see the incredible stories you’ll create with our new Enriched and Advanced UI for Web Stories!

title: How to enable IFTTT to automate share/post articles on social platforms
description:
We can automate the process of sharing/post articles on social media platforms using IFTTT through RSS Feed. Below are the following steps for the same


Step 1


Create/ Sign-in to the IFTTT account and click create









Step 2


Click Add for "If This" as shown below



Step 3


Type RSS -> Select RSS Feed

Step 4


Select "New feed item"   




Step 5


Give the RSS feed URL in the Feed URL as shown below. It can be a 

  • Generic RSS Feed URL  - https://demo.thepublive.com/rss
    • This will post whenever any article is published in the website
  • Category level RSS Feed URL - https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/categories/news. 
    • This will post whenever any article is published in that specific category  


Click Create Trigger


Step 6


Now, click Add to add the social platform in which the post needs to be shared



Step 7


Search for social platform and click the service available 


Here, we are adding Facebook service (Facebook Pages) 



Step 8


Select Create a link post  


Click Connect and sign in to the Facebook account





















Step 9


Click Add Ingredient in Link URL field and select EntryURL & any ingredients available -> Click Create Action -> Continue to next page 



For example, if you select Add ingredient, you can pick something like EntryTitle to show the title of the feed item in the post. Can add message if required but not mandatory. This will post the article url with the image thumbnail in the Facebook   


Step 10


Rename the service/applet if required and optionally enable notifications each time it runs, and then press Finish. 




Similar to Facebook, other social media platforms, can be enabled in the same way


Other Reference Resources


https://www.lifewire.com/set-up-rss-feed-on-facebook-3486528

title: How to Add your website to Google News Publication Center
description:
Following are the steps to add your website to the Google publisher center


Step 1

  • Go to Google Publisher Center and enter the website URL and click 'Add' if this is the first time adding a website to the publication center



  • Else click 'Other Publication' from the top right dropdown as shown in the screenshot below 

 


Step 2

  • Add your Website Name, URL, and Location in the pop-up window and click Add Publication


Step 3

  • You will now see your website visible on the top right being added 
  • As a next step, click Open under Google News 



Step 4

  • Click Edit and start filling in the details


Step 5


Under General, select the appropriate publication category, country, and google properties (default value - allow all properties) and click Next




Step 6


In Content settings, click New Section to add a category and corresponding RSS feed



Step 7


Provide the following,

  • Section Title - Category/tag name/generic/featured/any
  • RSS feed - corresponding category/tag/generic/featured RSS feed URL
    • Category: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/categories/category_slug
    • Tag: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/tags/tag_slug
    • Generic: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/
    • Featured: https://demo.thepublive.com/rss/featured/
  •  View access
    • default value "Anyone"

Then, Click Add



Similarly, add other categories as per your requirement in the above way



After some time, all articles will be available under each category RSS feed. Click refresh to see the changes


Note: After adding RSS feed URL, it may take some time for articles to be listed

 



Step 8

Next, go to the Review and Publish section. Click Review as shown in the screenshot below



Enter your name, email id, title(founder/director..etc), and organization in the pop-up form and select the checkbox and click Submit





Step 9


After completing all the above steps, click Publish

 


Once processing is completed, your website will be visible in Google News indicated as Live



Note: Once your account is Live, kindly share the Google News listing page URL with us to enable the Google News follow us button in your website




Reference

  • https://kinsta.com/blog/submit-to-google-news/
  • https://support.google.com/news/publisher-center/answer/9606481?hl=en

title: Using PubLive Newsletter functionality
description:
A powerful tool integrated with our CMS, enabling efficient distribution and tracking of newsletters. Enhance audience engagement, retain user loyalty, and explore new revenue streams through targeted content delivery and insightful analytics. Streamline your workflow and optimise the impact of your digital content strategy with this user-friendly, all-in-one solution.


Form Widget for your reader facing website


The Form Widget Display has always been a cornerstone of our newsletter feature, facilitating effortless integration of sign-up forms directly into user websites. 





Subscribers information on the backend: 



You can check their name, email address, and date on which they subscribed to the newsletter



Creating Newsletter Campaign


If you prefer not to publish this content on your website, you can create a newsletter instead. Simply select the 'Newsletter' option from the available formats after clicking on Create.



]When you select the ‘Newsletter’ option, the process is similar to creating and publishing an article. The key difference is that the content will not be published on your website. Instead, you can send it as a newsletter to your subscribers from the published section, as outlined below.



Similarly any published article can easily be sent as a newsletter with a click:


You can choose to send it to all subscribers/specific subscribers/selective newsletter groups:




Sample of published article shared as a newsletter through email: 




You can tick the box of ‘send teaser post’ to send the published article as a teaser : 



This helps to increase the views on your website as readers are redirected to your website if they want to read the entire article.



Sample of teaser post sent through email:


Multi-post Newsletter

This powerful tool is designed to streamline your email marketing efforts, allowing you to create, manage, and distribute multiple newsletter posts within a single campaign.

Given the crucial role of campaigns for publishers, this feature enables consistent communication with their audience, thereby enhancing engagement and retention. It streamlines content management by incorporating multiple posts into a single campaign. Furthermore, it offers detailed analytics, allowing publishers to refine their strategies based on performance insights.




Key Features

Multiple Posts

  • Effortless Management: Create several posts within one campaign, reducing the need for separate campaigns for each message.
  • Detailed Metrics: Track open rates, click-through rates, and engagement for each campaign.
  • Performance Insights: Compare the performance of different posts within the same campaign.

Benefits

  • Consistency: Ensure your messaging is consistent across multiple communications.
  • Efficiency: Save time by managing multiple posts within a single campaign.
  • Improved Engagement: Reach your audience with timely and relevant content

Templates

Here's a step-by-step guide to help you get started with Multi-Post Newsletter feature

Step 1: Selecting posts for Newsletter campaign 

  • To start using the Multi-Post Newsletter feature, navigate to the 'Published' section and select the corresponding posts

Step 2: Drafting and organizing the campaign

Click Send Newsletter after selecting the required set of posts

Step 3: Fill the Newsletter Details

Provide the required details for the campaign

  • Subject: Email subject
  • Message: Email body
  • Default Template: To ensure a professional standard look and feel
  • *Customized Template: Customize templates to match your brand’s identity and apply them across multiple posts

You can review the selected posts as shown below. Also, can add more posts using "Add more post" option

Step 4: Review the order of posts

Drag and drop for re-arranging the order of the posts if required. Can also delete the posts if found irrelevant for the campaign.

Once done, click Next to complete the Distribution settings.

Step 5: Distribution settings

Provide the required distribution details for the campaign

  • Choose Recipients: Select this checkbox if the campaign is to be sent to all publishers
  • Select Newsletter Group: To send a campaign for a specific newsletter group
  • Specific Email: To send a campaign to the specific email ID

Once done, click save to send the newsletter


Step 6: Mobile and Desktop view of the Newsletter


Step 7: Keep track of the performance of the campaigns

  • Accessing Analytics: Go to the 'Configuration' section -> Newsletter.
  • Viewing Metrics: Review key metrics such as open rates, click-through rates, and engagements for each campaign.

Best Practices

  • Consistency in Messaging: Maintain consistent messaging and branding across all posts.

  • Optimal Timing: Send the newsletter at times when your audience is most likely to engage.
  • Incorporating Feedback: Use feedback from analytics to refine future posts and campaigns.


How it benefits the digital publishers:

  1. Consistent Communication: The Multi-Post Newsletter allows digital publishers to maintain regular communication with their audience, fostering stronger engagement and loyalty.

  5. Strategic Refinement: Publishers can use detailed performance data to refine and optimize their content strategies, ensuring they meet audience preferences and improve future campaigns.
  6. Consistent Branding: Using predefined and customizable templates ensures a consistent look and feel across all posts, reinforcing brand identity and professionalism.

 

The Multi-Post Newsletter feature is a powerful tool for creating and managing comprehensive email campaigns. By leveraging this feature, users can ensure consistent and effective communication with their audience. We are confident that this new feature will significantly enhance your email marketing campaigns and help you connect more effectively with your audience. title: How Push Campaign works
description:
Push Campaign feature is available in PubLive dashboard. It is a proactive marketing approach that involves sending targeted messages or notifications directly to users' devices, aiming to engage them with relevant content, promotions, or updates without requiring their active request or interaction.


Following are the steps that can guide you on the same

 

Step 1: To send the push notification, go to the Published section. Click the bell icon available in extended menu visible under the Action column as shown   


This way, you can send the push notification for any published articles where the subscribed readers will receive the notification for that specific article as shown below

. 
Step 2: To Track the campaign, go to the Push campaign section under configuration in the dashboard. You can get the metrics on active subscriber count, total delivered notifications, and click rate for each notification campaign.
 



Below shown, the bell icon visible for the readers to subscribe to your website to receive push notifications.

Desktop 


Mobile 


Benefits of Push Campaign

  • Instant Communication: Direct and immediate channel for timely updates.
  • Increased Engagement: Boosts user interaction rates significantly.
  • Enhanced Retention: Reminds users and reduces churn rates.
  • Personalization: Tailors messages to user preferences for higher resonance.
  • Cost-Effectiveness: Efficient marketing strategy with minimal additional costs.
  • Automation: Saves time with automated delivery based on triggers.
  • Analytics and Optimization: Tracks performance for continuous improvement.
  • Cross-Platform Reach: Delivers notifications across various devices and platforms.
  • Customer Engagement: Strengthens relationships, fostering loyalty.
  • Promotion and Monetization: Drives traffic and revenue through targeted promotions.

title: Introducing the Multi-Post Newsletter Feature
description:
We are excited to announce the launch of our new Multi-Post Newsletter feature! This powerful tool is designed to streamline your email marketing efforts, allowing you to create, manage, and distribute multiple newsletter posts within a single campaign.

Given the crucial role of campaigns for publishers, this feature enables consistent communication with their audience, thereby enhancing engagement and retention. It streamlines content management by incorporating multiple posts into a single campaign. Furthermore, it offers detailed analytics, allowing publishers to refine their strategies based on performance insights.

Key Features

Multiple Posts

  • Effortless Management: Create several posts within one campaign, reducing the need for separate campaigns for each message.
  • Detailed Metrics: Track open rates, click-through rates, and engagement for each campaign.
  • Performance Insights: Compare the performance of different posts within the same campaign.

Benefits

  • Consistency: Ensure your messaging is consistent across multiple communications.
  • Efficiency: Save time by managing multiple posts within a single campaign.
  • Improved Engagement: Reach your audience with timely and relevant content

Templates

  • Default Template: To ensure a professional standard look and feel.
  • Customized Template: Customize templates to match your brand’s identity and apply them across multiple posts.

Getting Started

Here's a step-by-step guide to help you get started with Multi-Post Newsletter feature

Step 1: Selecting posts for Newsletter campaign 

  • To start using the Multi-Post Newsletter feature, navigate to the 'Published' section and select the corresponding posts

Step 2: Drafting and organizing the campaign

Click Send Newsletter after selecting the required set of posts

Step 3: Fill the Newsletter Details

Provide the required details for the campaign

  • Subject: Email subject
  • Message: Email body
  • Default Template: To ensure a professional standard look and feel
  • *Customized Template: Customize templates to match your brand’s identity and apply them across multiple posts

You can review the selected posts as shown below. Also, can add more posts using "Add more post" option

Step 4: Review the order of posts

Drag and drop for re-arranging the order of the posts if required. Can also delete the posts if found irrelevant for the campaign.

Once done, click Next to complete the Distribution settings.

Step 5: Distribution settings

Provide the required distribution details for the campaign

  • Choose Recipients: Select this checkbox if the campaign is to be sent to all publishers
  • Select Newsletter Group: To send a campaign for a specific newsletter group
  • Specific Email: To send a campaign to the specific email ID

Once done, click save to send the newsletter

Step 6: Mobile and Desktop view of the Newsletter

Step 7: Keep track of the performance of the campaigns

  • Accessing Analytics: Go to the 'Configuration' section -> Newsletter.
  • Viewing Metrics: Review key metrics such as open rates, click-through rates, and engagements for each campaign.
  • Comparing Performance: Analyze and compare the performance of different posts.

Best Practices

  • Consistency in Messaging: Maintain consistent messaging and branding across all posts.
  • Engaging Content: Use rich media such as images and videos to increase engagement.
  • Optimal Timing: Send the newsletter at times when your audience is most likely to engage.
  • Incorporating Feedback: Use feedback from analytics to refine future posts and campaigns.

How it benefits the digital publishers:

  1. Consistent Communication: The Multi-Post Newsletter allows digital publishers to maintain regular communication with their audience, fostering stronger engagement and loyalty.
  2. Streamlined Content Management: By integrating multiple posts into a single campaign, this feature simplifies content management, saving time and reducing the complexity of handling separate campaigns.
  3. Enhanced Audience Engagement: Scheduled and consistent updates keep the audience informed and engaged, increasing readership and interaction with the content.
  4. Detailed Analytics: The feature provides comprehensive analytics on each post's performance, offering insights into open rates, click-through rates, and overall engagement.
  5. Strategic Refinement: Publishers can use detailed performance data to refine and optimize their content strategies, ensuring they meet audience preferences and improve future campaigns.
  6. Consistent Branding: Using predefined and customizable templates ensures a consistent look and feel across all posts, reinforcing brand identity and professionalism.

 

The Multi-Post Newsletter feature is a powerful tool for creating and managing comprehensive email campaigns. By leveraging this feature, users can ensure consistent and effective communication with their audience. We are confident that this new feature will significantly enhance your email marketing campaigns and help you connect more effectively with your audience. Thank you for your continued support and feedback.

title: Understanding Bounce Rate Errors in Your Email Newsletters
description:
What is Bounce Rate?

Bounce rate refers to the percentage of emails that could not be delivered to the recipient’s inbox. These emails are "bounced back" by the receiving mail server and are usually caused by invalid or unreachable email addresses.


What’s a Healthy Bounce Rate?

A healthy bounce rate is below 2%. Higher rates can:

  • Harm your sender reputation
  • Reduce email deliverability
  • Get your domain or IP flagged as spam

We recommend regular list cleanup to keep your bounce rate low.

What Do Bounce Errors Mean?

Here are the most common bounce subtypes and diagnostic messages, with plain-language explanations and what you should do.


Bounce SubtypeDiagnostic Code / Message (Example)What It MeansWhat You Should Do
OnAccountSuppressionListAmazon SES did not send the message to this address because it is on the suppression list...This email address previously hard bounced or marked messages as spam. It’s now automatically suppressed by Amazon SES.Review and remove these addresses from your mailing list. See SES Suppression List Guide if needed.
MailboxNotFoundsmtp; 550-5.1.1 The email account you tried to reach does not exist.The email address doesn’t exist — likely due to a typo, fake address, or deleted account.Remove the address from your list. If input came from a form, enable double opt-in to prevent typos.
MailboxInactivesmtp; 550-5.2.1 The email account you tried to reach is inactive.The email account exists but is temporarily disabled or abandoned.Wait and retry once if expected to reactivate. Otherwise, remove it.
MailboxUnavailablesmtp; 550 Requested action not taken: mailbox unavailableThe mailbox is currently unavailable, possibly due to technical or server issues.Retry a few times. If it continues to bounce, treat it as a hard bounce and remove.
MailboxFull    smtp; 552 5.2.2 The email account is over quota.The recipient's mailbox is full and can’t accept new messages.

Retry later. If the bounce persists over multiple days, consider removing or flagging.


InvalidRecipient / UserNotFoundsmtp; 550 5.1.1 <email@example.com>: user does not existThe domain exists, but the specific user doesn't. This often occurs when employees leave companies.Remove from list. Optionally flag for manual review if it's a known contact.
Typo / Format Issuesmtp; ... double-checking the recipient's email address for typos or unnecessary spacesThe address may be malformed — extra characters, wrong domain, etc.Check for typos or cleanup issues. Remove if not fixable.
DomainDoesNotExistsmtp; 552 1 Requested mail action aborted, mailbox not foundThe entire domain (after @) does not exist.Remove from list immediately. May be a fake or mistyped domain.
Unknown Google Usersmtp; 550-5.1.1 ... <https://support.google.com/mail/?p=NoSuchUser>Google is reporting that the user does not exist.Remove from list. Cross-check if it's a valid user from your records.
Disabled Google Usersmtp; 550-5.2.1 ... <https://support.google.com/mail/?p=DisabledUser>The Gmail account is inactive or disabled by the user or Google.Remove from list unless you expect the user to reactivate their account.

What Should I Do With Bounced Emails?


Here’s how to handle bounced addresses on your end:

Clean Up Your List

  • ❌ Remove any email flagged as "does not exist", "inactive", or "suppressed".
  • ✅ Enable double opt-in so users confirm their email is correct.
  • Use an email validation tool to pre-check for invalid or inactive domains.

Avoid Risky Practices

  • Don’t buy email lists.
  • Don’t send to old/inactive lists without reconfirmation.
  • Avoid sending repeated emails to previously bounced addresses.

Why This Matters

A high bounce rate can cause:

  • Poor inbox placement
  • Delivery throttling or blacklisting
  • Messages going straight to spam

Keeping your list clean improves engagement and protects your brand reputation.

Need Help?

If you're unsure how to interpret a bounce error, or want help cleaning your list, feel free to contact our support team with:

  • The full error message
  • Bounce subtype (if available)
  • Email address in question

These emails are "bounced back" by the receiving mail server and are usually caused by invalid or unreachable email addresses.     What’s a Healthy Bounce Rate?  A healthy bounce rate is below 2%. Higher rates can:   Harm your sender reputation  Reduce email deliverability  Get your domain or IP flagged as spam   We recommend regular list cleanup to keep your bounce rate low.
