This email address previously hard bounced or marked messages as spam. It’s now automatically suppressed by Amazon SES.  Review and remove these addresses from your mailing list. See SES Suppression List Guide if needed.  MailboxNotFound  smtp; 550-5.1.1 The email account you tried to reach does not exist.  The email address doesn’t exist — likely due to a typo, fake address, or deleted account.  Remove the address from your list. If input came from a form, enable double opt-in to prevent typos.  MailboxInactive  smtp; 550-5.2.1 The email account you tried to reach is inactive.  The email account exists but is temporarily disabled or abandoned.  Wait and retry once if expected to reactivate. Otherwise, remove it.  MailboxUnavailable  smtp; 550 Requested action not taken: mailbox unavailable  The mailbox is currently unavailable, possibly due to technical or server issues.  Retry a few times. If it continues to bounce, treat it as a hard bounce and remove.  MailboxFull      smtp; 552 5.2.2 The email account is over quota.  The recipient's mailbox is full and can’t accept new messages.    Retry later. If the bounce persists over multiple days, consider removing or flagging.    InvalidRecipient / UserNotFound  smtp; 550 5.1.1 <email@example.com>: user does not exist  The domain exists, but the specific user doesn't. This often occurs when employees leave companies.  Remove from list. Optionally flag for manual review if it's a known contact. title: Domain Verification on Publive
description:
To send newsletters through Publive, you’ll need to verify your domain by configuring DNS records. This ensures that emails are delivered securely and are less likely to land in spam.

Why Domain Verification Matters

When you send newsletters from Publive, we want your readers to trust that emails actually come from your domain. Domain verification helps with:

  • SPF (Sender Policy Framework)
    Prevents spoofing by telling recipient servers which mail servers are authorized to send on behalf of your domain.

  • DKIM (DomainKeys Identified Mail)
    Adds a cryptographic signature to every email, so recipients can confirm the email hasn’t been altered and is really from your domain.

  • DMARC (Domain-based Message Authentication, Reporting, and Conformance)
    Builds on SPF and DKIM. Lets you define how recipient servers handle emails that fail authentication (monitor, quarantine, or reject).


✅ Together, these improve deliverability, protect your brand reputation, and keep newsletters out of spam.

First-Time Setup in Publive

  1. Go to Integrations

    • From the Publive dashboard, click Configuration > Integrations.

    • Locate the Newsletter Setup card and click Set Up.

  2. Enter Your Sender Email

    • Add the email address you want to send newsletters from (e.g., news@yourdomain.com).

    • Publive will detect the domain (yourdomain.com) and begin the verification process.

  3. View DNS Records

    • Publive will generate the required SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records for your domain.

    • You’ll see these in a table with copy buttons for easy setup.

Adding Records in Your DNS Provider

Example is of Cloudflare, but steps are similar for GoDaddy, Namecheap, or others.

  1. Log in to your DNS provider.

  2. Select your domain.

  3. Go to the DNS Records section.

  4. Click Add Record.

    • For SPF/DKIM: Choose TXT.

    • For DMARC: Choose TXT with host _dmarc.

    • Enter the Host/Name and Value/Content exactly as shown in Publive.

  5. Save changes.

⏳ DNS propagation may take up to 24 hours.

Verifying Records in Publive

  1. After adding records, return to Publive’s setup page.

  2. Click Verify DNS Records.

  3. Status will update from Pending → Verified once propagation completes.

    • If verified, you’ll see green checkmarks next to each record.

    • If still pending, wait a few hours and try again.

Troubleshooting

  • Not verified after 24 hours?

    • Double-check you copied Host and Value correctly.

    • Ensure there are no duplicate SPF records (only one allowed per domain).

    • Make sure DMARC host is _dmarc, not your full domain.

  • Still stuck?
    Reach out to your DNS provider support or contact Publive support with a screenshot of your DNS records.

Next Steps

Once your domain is verified:

  • You’re ready to send newsletters from your domain.

  • Test by sending a preview email to your personal inbox.

  • Monitor your deliverability and bounce rates from the Analytics tab.

That’s it! Your domain is now authenticated, ensuring higher trust and better inbox placement for your newsletters.


❓ Frequently Asked Questions

1. Can I use a Gmail/Yahoo/Outlook address instead of my domain email?
No. Free mailbox providers (like Gmail, Yahoo, Outlook) do not allow adding custom SPF/DKIM/DMARC records. You must use a custom domain email (e.g., news@yourdomain.com).

2. Can I verify more than one domain?
Yes. Publive supports multiple verified domains. Each newsletter campaign must be sent from one verified domain.

3. How long does verification take?
Usually within minutes, but DNS changes can take up to 24 hours to propagate worldwide.

4. What if I already have SPF/DKIM/DMARC records?
You can merge Publive’s required values with your existing records:

  • SPF → Only one SPF record is allowed. Combine values into a single TXT record.

  • DKIM → Multiple DKIM records are fine; just add Publive’s.

  • DMARC → You can only have one DMARC policy; adjust it if needed.

5. Do I need to update records again later?
No. Once verified, you don’t need to update unless you change DNS providers or domains.

6. What happens if I don’t set up domain verification?
You won’t be able to send newsletters from Publive. Even if attempted, unauthenticated emails are more likely to be rejected or marked as spam.

title: Turn Readership into Revenue with Our Paywall Integration
description:
Empower your publication with our Paywall Configuration feature, designed to effectively monetize content through subscription-based or one-time payment models. Ensure high-quality, exclusive content for readers while generating sustainable revenue.

Key Features

Flexible Paywall Models

  • Hard Paywall: Restricts access to all content, requiring a subscription to view articles.
  • Freemium Model: Provides free and premium content, with premium content available only to subscribers.

Customizable Access Rules

  • Set access rules based on categories & content type.
  • Offer different subscription tiers with varying levels of access.

Payment Integration

  • Currently, Integration is supported with Razorpay for seamless transactions.
  • Support for recurring billing and one-time payments based on the model publishers opt for 

User Management

  • Know your subscribers with a built-in user management system.
  • Track user activity and subscription status.

Content Preview

  • Provide enticing content previews to encourage subscriptions.

Responsive Design:

  • Optimized for both desktop and mobile users.
  • AMP compatibility for fast-loading pages on mobile devices.

Note: This feature is available for Advanced and Premium plans.


Get Started

Required Information for Paywall Setup

To set up the paywall, publishers need to provide the following information in the dashboard:

  • Merchant ID
  • API Key
  • API Secret
  • Plan Details: Plan Name, Amount, Duration (Time | Frequency)

This information is crucial for configuring the paywall and ensuring seamless integration with Razorpay for secure and efficient payment processing. Also, manual renewal and automatic subscription depend on what setting the publisher has opted for.


Razorpay Account Creation

[*Can skip this if an account already exists]

Razorpay is a robust payment gateway that integrates seamlessly with the paywall system, ensuring smooth and secure subscription transactions. For account setup, refer to the following guide: Razorpay Account Setup


Three Major Steps for Paywall Setup

  1. Integrating Razorpay Account with PubLive
  2. Customizing the Subscription Plan Page
  3. Setting Up a Paywall for Articles

 

1. Razorpay Account Integration in PubLive

To get started, login to PubLive Dashboard

Step 1: Connect Razorpay

Navigate to Configuration > Paywall Configuration > Connect.

Fetch the Merchant ID, API Key, API Secret from Razorpay 

Step 2: Retrieve Razorpay Credentials

Merchant ID

API Secret & Key

Navigate to Accounts & Settings > API Keys > Generate Key

Note down Key ID and Key Secret from the pop-up page

Step 3: Complete Setup 

Enter the credentials and click Connect.  

Existing plans in Razorpay will appear automatically. If not, set them up as needed in Razorpay, or we can assist from the backend.


2. Subscription Plan Page Customization

Once connected, customize the subscription plan page and models from the dashboard: 

Step 1: Paywall Configuration 

Go to Configurations -> Paywall Configurations

Click Edit to edit/update any plans

Review the following details 

  • Edit/Update plans (Plan Name, Plan Description, CTA Text, Plan Information)
  • Recurring Payment and Billing Frequency fields are pre-filled and non-editable from Razorpay
  • Review plan details using the plan preview on the right  
  • Once done, click save

Step 2: Paywall Design 

This tab allows you to customize the paywall modal that appears on article pages

  • Paywall Information: Enter the information to be displayed on the paywall modal.
  • Paywall Preview: See a preview of how the paywall will appear with the provided information.
  • Other Customizable Details:
    • Logo
    • Paywall background color
    • Paywall text color
    • Paywall button color
    • CTA text and CTA link
    • Optionally, add additional CTA buttons and links if needed

Step 3: Subscription Plans Landing Page Customization

Customize the subscription plans landing page using the following fields:

  • Header Information: Create an engaging and informative heading with subtext to clearly communicate your plans and essential details.
  • Plan Configuration: Simplify the configuration of your landing page by selecting and recommending plans to enhance user engagement.
  • Additional Information: Enhance the user experience by adding extra details and information, such as FAQs, to provide a comprehensive and informative landing page.


Header Information 

Craft a captivating and informative heading to clearly convey your plans and essential information to your readers.

Plan configuration 

Existing plans from Razorpay, as well as new ones created upon request by publishers, will be available for selection and display on the landing page.

On hover on plans, can highlight any plans as recommended for the readers as shown below

Additional Information

Enhance your landing page with extra details to provide a comprehensive and informative user experience. Use blocks to add FAQs and other intuitive information that may be helpful to readers.


Website | Landing Page 

All set now for the website, we will now set a paywall for the articles 

3. Setting up a paywall for the articles

Go to the Published section -> Edit the article

Set the Access level to Paid in the advanced Properties at the article level.

For more details on article-level access restrictions, visit: Article Level Access Restriction

Website | Paid Article

Readers will see the paywall at the bottom of the article. 

Clicking the CTA button takes them to the subscription plans landing page.

 Users will be prompted to log in if not already logged in and then taken to the plan page.

On selecting the plan, readers will be taken to payment gateway 

On payment successful, readers will be confirmed with payment completion

Razorpay sends payment receipt upon successful payment also for failed payments

Users will be redirected to the article page upon successful subscription.

Congratulations! Your paywall is now set up and ready, enabling you to attract readers and monetize your content effectively.

Benefits for Publishers

  1. Revenue Generation:

    • Monetize high-quality content directly from users, creating a steady revenue stream.
    • Diversify revenue sources beyond traditional advertising.

  2. Audience Insights:

    • Gain valuable insights into user behavior and preferences through detailed analytics.
    • Use data to refine content strategies and improve user engagement.

  3. Content Control:

    • Protect exclusive content and maintain its value by limiting access to paying subscribers.
    • Flexibility to experiment with different paywall models and find the optimal balance.

  4. Marketing Opportunities:

    • Use promotional tools to attract new subscribers and retain existing ones.
    • Implement targeted marketing campaigns based on user data and behavior.

  5. Sustainable Business Model:

    • Reduce reliance on volatile advertising revenue.
    • Build a sustainable business model focused on delivering value to subscribers.

  6. Scalability:

    • Easily scale the paywall system to accommodate growing audiences and expanding content libraries.
    • Integrate with other systems and platforms as needed.

  7. Brand Loyalty:

    • Strengthen brand loyalty by offering exclusive, high-quality content.
    • Create a community of engaged, paying users who are invested in the publisher’s success.

 

Start monetizing your content today with our new Paywall feature and unlock the full revenue potential of your articles. For more information and to get started, contact our support team with the requested details to setup the paywall.

title: How to Get Your Razorpay API Keys
description:
To integrate Razorpay with any platform, you’ll need to generate your API Key ID and API Key Secret from the Razorpay dashboard. Here’s how you can do it step by step:

Step-by-Step: Generate API Keys on Razorpay

1. Log in to Razorpay

  • Visit https://dashboard.razorpay.com

  • Enter your registered email and password to sign in.

2. Go to the API Keys Section

  • Once logged in, look at the left-hand sidebar.

  • Click on ⚙️ Settings

  • Under Settings, select “API Keys”

You can also view Razorpay’s official documentation here:? Razorpay API Keys Guide

3. Generate or Copy Keys

  • Click on “Generate Key”

  • Razorpay will display two values:

    • Key ID – Safe to share

    • Key Secret – Sensitive (copy immediately, shown only once)

⚠️ Important:
Make sure to copy and securely store the Key Secret. If you lose it, you’ll need to generate a new key pair.

Regenerating Keys

If your Key Secret was lost or compromised:

  • Go back to the API Keys section

  • Click “Regenerate Key”

  • A new Key ID + Key Secret will be created (your old keys will stop working)

That’s it! You now have your Razorpay API credentials ready to be used for integration.

title: How to use Typeform with PubLive
description:
The integration between PubLive and Typeform allows publishers to create engaging forms and surveys that can be seamlessly embedded into their PubLive pages. This integration enhances user interaction and data collection, making it easier to gather feedback, conduct surveys, and engage with your audience. 


About Typeform

Typeform is a versatile online tool that enables users to create interactive and visually appealing forms, surveys, quizzes, and more. Known for its user-friendly design and engaging interface, Typeform transforms data collection into a seamless and enjoyable experience for both creators and respondents.


Key Features

  1. Seamless Embedding:

    • Easily embed Typeform forms and surveys into your PubLive pages without any coding knowledge.
    • Customize the appearance to match your site’s design and branding.

  2. Interactive and Engaging Forms:

    • Use Typeform's interactive and visually appealing forms to enhance user engagement.
    • Incorporate various question types, including multiple choice, short answer, and rating scales.

  3. Real-Time Data Collection:

    • Collect responses in real time and view them directly within your Typeform dashboard.

  4. Enhanced User Experience:

    • Provide a smooth and engaging experience for your audience, encouraging higher response rates.
    • Utilize Typeform’s mobile-friendly design for seamless user interaction on any device.


Setup Instructions

Create a Typeform Account:

  • If you don’t already have a Typeform account, sign up at Typeform.

Create Your Form or Survey:

  • Design your form or survey using Typeform’s intuitive builder.
  • Customize the questions, layout, and design to match your requirements.

Get the Embed Code:

  • Once your form is ready, navigate to the “Share” tab of the form builder in Typeform.

  • Press the Get the code button and copy the embed code provided. 


Embed in PubLive:

  • Log in to your PubLive account.
  • Navigate to the page where you want to embed the form.
  • Use the HTML embed option by going to Insert -> Media -> Embed to paste the Typeform embed code into the desired location on your page. 


Publish and Test:

  • Save your changes and publish the page.
  • Test the embedded form to ensure it displays correctly and functions as intended.


Automation to submissions

title: Integration of Hotjar with PubLive
description:
PubLive now integrates with Hotjar, enabling you to gain deeper insights into user behavior on your website. Hotjar provides powerful tools for heatmaps, session recordings, surveys, and more, helping you understand how visitors interact with your content and identify areas for improvement.


Benefits

1. Heatmaps

  • Visualize where users click, move, and scroll on your pages.
  • Identify popular areas and elements that attract the most attention.
  • Optimize layout and design based on user interaction.

2. Session Recordings

  • Watch recordings of real visitor sessions to see how they navigate your site.
  • Understand user behavior and identify any usability issues.
  • Improve the user experience by making data-driven decisions.

3. Conversion Funnels

  • Track the steps users take towards a specific goal (e.g., signing up or making a purchase).
  • Identify where users drop off and optimize the funnel to increase conversions.

4. Feedback Polls and Surveys

  • Collect direct feedback from your users through on-site surveys and polls.
  • Understand user needs and preferences to tailor your content and features.
  • Measure user satisfaction and gather suggestions for improvement.

5. Behavioral Insights

  • Analyze user behavior to understand motivations and frustrations.
  • Identify trends and patterns to inform your content strategy.
  • Enhance the overall user experience by addressing pain points.


How to Set Up Hotjar Integration on PubLive

Sign Up for Hotjar

  • If you don't have a Hotjar account, sign up at Hotjar.

Get Your Hotjar Tracking Code

  • Once logged in, go to your Hotjar Dashboard
  • Click on “Tracking Code” and copy the code snippet provided

Add Hotjar Tracking Code to PubLive

  • Log in to your PubLive dashboard
  • Paste your Hotjar tracking code in the Code Injection Head field in page level and save



Verify Installation

  • Go back to your Hotjar Dashboard and verify that the tracking code is working
  • Start seeing data collected from your website


Conclusion

Integrating Hotjar with PubLive empowers you with valuable insights to improve user experience and optimize your website's performance. By leveraging heatmaps, session recordings, conversion funnels, and user feedback, you can make informed decisions to enhance your content and increase engagement.


For more detailed instructions and support, visit Hotjar's Help Center or contact our support team.

title: Integrating Microsoft Clarity with Publive: A Step-by-Step Guide
description:
PubLive now integrates with Microsoft Clarity, enabling you to gain deeper insights into user behavior on your website.  

What is Microsoft Clarity?

Microsoft Clarity is a free web analytics tool that provides deep insights into how users interact with your website. It offers features such as session recordings, heatmaps, and insightful data on user behavior, helping you understand how your audience navigates your content. Clarity's key benefits include:


  • Session Recordings: Watch recordings of real user sessions to see how visitors interact with your site, helping you identify any pain points.
  • Heatmaps: Visualize where users click, scroll, and move on your pages, allowing you to optimize your layout and design.
  • User Insights: Gain valuable insights into user behaviors, such as rage clicks, dead clicks, and excessive scrolling, to improve user experience.
  • GDPR and CCPA Compliance: Clarity is built with privacy in mind, ensuring that your data collection complies with major privacy regulations.


Benefits of Microsoft Clarity for Publive Users

  • Improved Content Strategy: Understand which sections of your content are capturing attention and which are being overlooked.
  • Enhanced User Experience: Identify and resolve issues that might be frustrating your visitors, such as slow-loading pages or confusing navigation.
  • Data-Driven Decisions: Use Clarity's insights to make informed decisions about your website design, content placement, and overall strategy.
  • Increased Engagement: By optimizing your site based on Clarity’s data, you can create a more engaging experience, leading to higher retention rates.


How to Integrate Microsoft Clarity with Publive

Step 1: Sign Up for Microsoft Clarity

        1. Go to the Microsoft Clarity website.

 

        2. Click on Get Started and sign in with your Microsoft account.

        3. Create a new project by entering your website's name and URL.

Step 2: Get the Clarity Tracking Code

  • Once your project is created, Clarity will generate a tracking code for your website.
  • Copy the tracking code, which you will need to add to your website.

 

Step 3: Add the Clarity Tracking Code to Publive

  1. Log in to your Publive dashboard.
  2. Share your Clarity tracking code with the PubLive Tech Team, will set this up for you in the Head or Footer section, depending on where you prefer the script to load.


Automation to submissions


To make your form more powerful, consider adding some automation by using Zapier to process your form submissions. There are tons of things you can do with it, but here is the link to help you get started


How to use PubLive and Zapier together? 

Zapier Integration

description:
Publishers can export published post data for the last 30 days, with options to set time frames and content types. Here are the following steps that can be followed to Export Data.

  • Reader Data: Export your reader data in csv format with ease with ease.


Step 3: Select your date range, choose your content type and hit Export Post

 

Step 4: You will receive E-mail regarding downloading the data file (csv format), click on Download button

title: Addressing "Not Indexed" Pages in Google Search Console
description:
This document provides guidance for publishers whose Google Search Console (GSC) reports show a significant number of pages not indexed due to the following reasons:


1. Crawled - currently not indexed

2. Discovered - currently not indexed

3. Alternative page with proper canonical tag

4. Not found (404)

5. Page with redirect

6. Excluded by 'noindex' tag


For each scenario, we’ll explore the causes, troubleshooting steps, and actionable solutions to help improve indexing.


Crawled - Currently Not Indexed

What It Means:

Google has crawled the page but has chosen not to index it. This typically happens when Google determines the page doesn’t provide enough value or relevance to be indexed.


Common Causes:

  • Low-quality or thin content.
  • Duplicate content without proper canonical tags.
  • Pages with little or no traffic or engagement.
  • Technical issues like slow loading times or poor mobile usability.


Steps to Resolve:


1. Evaluate Content Quality:

  • Ensure the page has unique, high-quality, and valuable content.
  • Avoid thin content or pages with little substantive information.


2. Check for Duplicate Content:

  • Use canonical tags to indicate the preferred version of duplicate pages.
  • Consolidate similar pages if necessary.


3. Improve Page Performance:

  • Optimize page speed and ensure mobile-friendliness.
  • Use tools like Google PageSpeed Insights to identify and fix performance issues.


4. Enhance Internal Linking:

  • Ensure the page is linked from other relevant pages on your site.
  • Use a clear site structure to help Google understand the page’s importance.


5. Monitor and Resubmit:

  • Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check the page’s status.
  • Request re-indexing after making improvements.


Discovered - Currently Not Indexed

What It Means:

Google has discovered the page (e.g., through sitemaps or internal links) but hasn’t crawled it yet. This often happens due to crawl budget limitations or low priority.


Common Causes:

  • Large websites with many low-priority pages.
  • Poor site architecture or lack of internal linking.
  • Crawl budget issues (common for very large sites).
  • Blocked by robots.txt or noindex tags (though this would typically show a different status).


Steps to Resolve:


1. Optimize Crawl Budget:

  • Prioritize important pages in your sitemap.
  • Remove or noindex low-value pages (e.g., filters, session IDs).


2. Improve Internal Linking:

  • Ensure important pages are linked from high-traffic pages.
  • Use a clear and logical site hierarchy.


3. Submit a Sitemap:

  • Create and submit an XML sitemap to GSC.
  • Regularly update the sitemap to reflect new or updated pages.


4. Check for Blocking Issues:

  • Ensure the page isn’t blocked by robots.txt or noindex tags.
  • Use the URL Inspection Tool to verify crawlability.


5. Monitor and Request Crawling:

  • Use the URL Inspection Tool to request indexing for important pages.
  • Be patient, as crawling may take time for large sites.


Alternative Page with Proper Canonical Tag

What It Means:

Google has identified another page as the canonical (preferred) version of the content, so the current page is not indexed. This is not necessarily an issue if the canonical tag is correctly implemented.    


Common Causes:

  • The page is a duplicate or near-duplicate of another page.
  • The canonical tag points to another URL.
  • The page is intentionally marked as non-canonical.


Steps to Resolve:

1. Verify Canonical Tags:

  • Ensure the canonical tag points to the correct preferred version of the page.
  • Use the URL Inspection Tool to check canonicalization.


2. Consolidate Duplicate Content:

  • If the page is a duplicate, consider merging it with the canonical version.
  • Redirect the non-canonical page to the canonical version if appropriate.


3. Check for Conflicting Signals:

  • Ensure there are no conflicting signals (e.g., hreflang tags pointing to different pages).
  • Use consistent internal linking to the canonical page.


4. Monitor Indexing Status:

  • Use GSC to monitor the indexing status of the canonical page.
  • Ensure the canonical page is indexed and performing well.


5. Reassess Content Strategy:

  • If the page is intentionally non-canonical, ensure it serves a purpose (e.g., for internal use or specific audiences).
  • Consider removing or noindexing pages that don’t need to be indexed.


General Best Practices for All Scenarios:


Regularly Audit Your Site: Use tools like Screaming Frog or Sitebulb to identify technical issues.

  • Monitor GSC Reports: Keep an eye on the Index Coverage report for trends and anomalies.
  • Focus on Quality: Prioritize high-quality, unique, and valuable content.
  • Stay Updated: Follow Google’s guidelines and algorithm updates to ensure compliance.


Not found (404)

What It Means:

A 404 error indicates that the page no longer exists or cannot be found on your website. This happens when:

  • The page has been deleted.
  • The URL has been changed without a proper redirect.
  • There are broken internal or external links pointing to the page.


Steps to Resolve:

Step 1: Identify the Cause

  • Use GSC’s Coverage Report to find URLs with 404 errors.
  • Check if the page was intentionally removed or if it’s a broken link.

Step 2: Fix the Issue

  1. Restore the Page (if needed):
    • If the page was accidentally deleted, restore it from backups or recreate it.
    • Ensure the URL matches the original.
  2. Fix Broken Links:
    • Use tools like Screaming Frog or Ahrefs to find and fix broken internal links.
    • Update or remove external links pointing to the 404 page.

Step 3: Monitor and Validate

  • Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check if the 404 error is resolved.
  • Regularly monitor the Coverage Report for new 404 errors.

Page with Redirect

What It Means:

This error occurs when a page has a redirect (e.g., 301 or 302) instead of serving the content directly. While redirects are not inherently bad, they can cause issues if:

  • There are multiple redirects (redirect chains).
  • The redirect points to an irrelevant or broken page.
  • The redirect is unnecessary.


Steps to Resolve:

Step 1: Identify the Redirect

  • Use GSC’s Coverage Report to find URLs with redirects.
  • Use tools like Screaming Frog or Redirect Path (Chrome extension) to analyze redirect chains.

Step 2: Fix the Issue

  1. Remove Unnecessary Redirects:
    • If the redirect is unnecessary, remove it and serve the content directly.
  2. Shorten Redirect Chains:
    • Ensure there are no more than one or two redirects in a chain.
    • Directly redirect the old URL to the final destination.
  3. Update Internal Links:
    • Update internal links to point to the final destination URL instead of the redirected URL.
  4. Check External Links:
    • If possible, ask external websites to update their links to the final destination.

Step 3: Monitor and Validate

  • Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check if the redirect issue is resolved.
  • Regularly audit your site for redirect chains.

Excluded by Noindex Tags

This error occurs when a page is excluded from indexing because it has a noindex tag. This tag tells search engines not to index the page. Common causes include:

  • The page was intentionally marked as noindex like reader login page, no result found page (404).
  • If the tag page contains less than 4 posts it will be mark as noindex.
  • The page is a duplicate or low-priority page.


Steps to Resolve:

Step 1: Identify the Cause

  • Use GSC’s Coverage Report to find URLs excluded by noindex tags.
  • Check the page’s HTML source code or HTTP headers for the noindex tag.

Step 2: Fix the Issue

  1. Remove the Noindex Tag (if needed):
    • If the page should be indexed, remove the noindex tag.
    • Example: Remove <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> from the page’s <head> section.
  2. Check for Conflicting Signals:
    • Ensure the page is not blocked by robots.txt or other directives.
    • Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check for crawlability.
  3. Reindex the Page:
    • Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to request indexing after removing the noindex tag.
  4. Audit Low-Priority Pages:
    • If the page is intentionally noindexed, ensure it serves a purpose (e.g., thank-you pages, internal tools).

Step 3: Monitor and Validate

  • Use GSC’s Coverage Report to confirm the page is no longer excluded.
  • Monitor the page’s indexing status and traffic.

General Best Practices

Regular Audits: Use tools like Screaming Frog, Ahrefs, or SEMrush to audit your site for errors.

Monitor GSC Reports: Regularly check the Coverage Report and Indexing Report in GSC.

Fix Errors Promptly: Address errors as soon as they are detected to avoid negative impacts on SEO.


Useful Resources:

  • Crawled - currently not indexed
  • Discovered - currently not indexed

  • Alternative page with proper canonical tag

  • Google’s Guide to Title Tags and Meta Descriptions
  • Keyword Placement Best Practices
  • Internal Linking
  • Crawlability and Indexing
  • XML Sitemaps
  • Google Search Console URL Inspection Tool
  • Google’s SEO Starter Guide
  • Google Search Central - Noindex
  • Google Search Central - Coverage Report


By addressing the specific reasons for "Not Indexed" pages and implementing the recommended solutions, publishers can improve their site’s indexing and overall search performance. Regularly monitor GSC and make adjustments as needed to ensure optimal visibility in search results.

Fix Broken Links:  Use tools like Screaming Frog or Ahrefs to find and fix broken internal links.  Update or remove external links pointing to the 404 page.     Step 3: Monitor and Validate   Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check if the 404 error is resolved.  Regularly monitor the Coverage Report for new 404 errors.   Page with Redirect   What It Means:  This error occurs when a page has a redirect (e.g., 301 or 302) instead of serving the content directly. While redirects are not inherently bad, they can cause issues if:   There are multiple redirects (redirect chains).  The redirect points to an irrelevant or broken page.  The redirect is unnecessary.      Steps to Resolve:  Step 1: Identify the Redirect   Use GSC’s Coverage Report to find URLs with redirects.  Use tools like Screaming Frog or Redirect Path (Chrome extension) to analyze redirect chains.   Step 2: Fix the Issue   Remove Unnecessary Redirects:  If the redirect is unnecessary, remove it and serve the content directly.    Shorten Redirect Chains:  Ensure there are no more than one or two redirects in a chain.  Directly redirect the old URL to the final destination.    Update Internal Links:  Update internal links to point to the final destination URL instead of the redirected URL.    Check External Links:  If possible, ask external websites to update their links to the final destination.     Step 3: Monitor and Validate   Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check if the redirect issue is resolved.  Regularly audit your site for redirect chains.   Excluded by Noindex Tags  This error occurs when a page is excluded from indexing because it has a noindex tag. This tag tells search engines not to index the page. Common causes include:   The page was intentionally marked as noindex like reader login page, no result found page (404).  If the tag page contains less than 4 posts it will be mark as noindex.  The page is a duplicate or low-priority page.      Steps to Resolve:  Step 1: Identify the Cause   Use GSC’s Coverage Report to find URLs excluded by noindex tags.  Check the page’s HTML source code or HTTP headers for the noindex tag.   Step 2: Fix the Issue   Remove the Noindex Tag (if needed):  If the page should be indexed, remove the noindex tag.  Example: Remove <meta name="robots" content="noindex"> from the page’s <head> section.    Check for Conflicting Signals:  Ensure the page is not blocked by robots.txt or other directives.  Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to check for crawlability.    Reindex the Page:  Use GSC’s URL Inspection Tool to request indexing after removing the noindex tag.    Audit Low-Priority Pages:  If the page is intentionally noindexed, ensure it serves a purpose (e.g., thank-you pages, internal tools).     Step 3: Monitor and Validate   Use GSC’s Coverage Report to confirm the page is no longer excluded.  Monitor the page’s indexing status and traffic.   General Best Practices  Regular Audits: Use tools like Screaming Frog, Ahrefs, or SEMrush to audit your site for errors.  Monitor GSC Reports: Regularly check the Coverage Report and Indexing Report in GSC.  Fix Errors Promptly: Address errors as soon as they are detected to avoid negative impacts on SEO.     Useful Resources:   Crawled - currently not indexed  Discovered - currently not indexed   Alternative page with proper canonical tag    Google’s Guide to Title Tags and Meta Descriptions  Keyword Placement Best Practices  Internal Linking  Crawlability and Indexing  XML Sitemaps  Google Search Console URL Inspection Tool  Google’s SEO Starter Guide  Google Search Central - Noindex  Google Search Central - Coverage Report      By addressing the specific reasons for "Not Indexed" pages and implementing the recommended solutions, publishers can improve their site’s indexing and overall search performance. Regularly monitor GSC and make adjustments as needed to ensure optimal visibility in search results.

title: Common AMP Issues, Causes, and Solutions
description:
AMP (Accelerated Mobile Pages) is a framework that enforces strict guidelines to ensure high-speed performance and smooth rendering of web pages. However, certain issues commonly occur due to violations of AMP’s rules. This document outlines common AMP errors, their

causes, and solutions, along with best practices to prevent them.


1. Missing URL in HTML tag <img>

Cause:

  • The <img> tag lacks a src attribute or contains an empty value.
  • The src URL is dynamically inserted via JavaScript, which is not allowed in AMP.


Solution:

  • Ensure every <img> tag has a valid src attribute.
  • Use <amp-img> instead of <img>:
    Tag sample:
    <amp-img src="https://example.com/image.jpg" width="600" height="400"

    layout="responsive"></amp-img>


Prevention:

  • Always specify a valid src in <amp-img>.
  • Validate the page using AMP Validator.


2. Custom JavaScript is Not Allowed

Cause:

  • AMP does not support traditional JavaScript (<script> tags) for security and performance reasons.


Solution:

  • Use AMP components instead, such as:
    <amp-carousel> for sliders
    <amp-bind> for interactivity
    <amp-analytics> for tracking.


Prevention:

  • Replace standard JavaScript functionalities with AMP components.


3. Disallowed Attribute or Attribute Value Present in HTML Tag

Cause:

  • Use of non-AMP attributes (e.g., onclick, onmouseover).


Solution:

  • Remove non-AMP attributes or use AMP alternatives:
    Tag Sample:
    <button on="tap:AMP.setState({modalOpen: true})">Click Me</button>


Prevention:

  • Refer to the AMP Attribute Guidelines before implementation.


4. Only amp-boilerplate and amp-custom <style> Tags Are Allowed

Cause:

  • Custom styles are placed outside <style amp-custom>.


Solution:

  • Move all custom CSS inside <style amp-custom>:
    Code Sample:
    <style amp-custom>

             body { background: #fff; }

    </style>


Prevention:

  • Make sure all custom styles are placed within <style amp-custom> and avoid using external CSS files.


5. A Mandatory Attribute is Missing from an HTML Tag

Cause:

  • Certain AMP elements require mandatory attributes (e.g., <amp-img> needs width, height, and layout).


Solution:

  • Add the missing attributes:
    Tag Sample:
    <amp-img src="image.jpg" width="500" height="300" layout="responsive"></amp-img>


Prevention:

  • Follow the AMP Component Documentation.


6. CSS Syntax Error in <span> or <style amp-custom>

Cause:

  • Invalid CSS rules inside <style amp-custom>


Solution:

  • Remove or correct invalid CSS properties.


Prevention:

  • Use AMP CSS Validator to verify correctness.


7. Usage of !important CSS Qualifier is Not Allowed

Cause:

  • AMP prohibits !important due to style manipulation restrictions.


Solution:

  • Remove !important and adjust styles accordingly.


Prevention:

  • Adhere to AMP CSS Restrictions


8. A Tag on This Page Requires an AMP Component <script> Tag, Which is Missing

Cause:

  • Missing <script> for AMP components used (e.g., <amp-carousel> needs amp-carousel script)


Solution:

  • Include the required <script> tag inside <head>:
    Tag Sample:
    <script async custom-element="amp-carousel" src="https://cdn.ampproject.org/v0/amp-carousel-0.2.js"></script>


Prevention:

  • Check required scripts for each AMP component.


9. Invalid URL Protocol in src or href Attribute

Cause:

  • Non-secure URLs (http:// instead of https://)


Solution:

  • Ensure all URLs use the secure HTTPS protocol to comply with AMP guidelines.


Prevention:

  • Enforce https:// for all resources


10. The Parent Tag of <picture> Must be <noscript>

Cause:

  • <picture> is used incorrectly in AMP


Solution:

  • Wrap <picture> inside <noscript>:
    Tag Sample:
    <noscript>
    <picture>
    <source srcset="image.jpg" type="image/webp">
    </picture>
    </noscript>


Prevention:

  • Follow AMP Guidelines for Images.


11. Tag <amp-story> Has a Disallowed Child Tag

Cause:

  • Invalid child elements inside <amp-story>


Solution:

  • Use only allowed elements such as <amp-story-page>, <amp-story-grid-layer>, and <amp-img>


Prevention:

  • Review the AMP Story Documentation


12. Malformed URL Found in href Attribute of <a> Tag

Cause:

  • The href attribute contains an invalid URL, such as missing a protocol or malformed structure.


Solution:

  • Ensure the URL in href is properly formatted, including https://
    Tag Sample:
    <a href="https://example.com">Valid Link</a>


Prevention:

  • Validate URLs before adding them to <a> tags
  • Use AMP Validator to check for errors.


By following these best practices and using AMP-compliant elements, you can prevent

common AMP errors, improve website performance, and maintain a smooth user experience.

Always validate your AMP pages using:

  • AMP Validator
  • Google Search Console


For more details, refer to the AMP Official Documentation.

title: Giving Access In Google Analytics
description:
To give access to your Google Analytics account, you can follow these steps:



 

Step 1

  • Sign in to your Google Analytics account
  • Click on the Admin tab in the navigation menu


Step 2

  • Under the "Account" section, select the account that you want to give access to
  • Click “Account User Management.”


Step 3

  • Click the blue  +  to add a new user to your account
  • Enter the email address
  • Select "Notify new users by email" for the person to receive the invite
  • Set roles as shown below for the member


Here are the options

  • Administrator: Grants complete control over the Google Analytics dashboard, from the account, property, and view levels
  • Editor: Allows the user to edit anything within the account, except for user settings
  • Analyst: Works like a Viewer role (below), but with the ability to make and edit shared items like dashboards
  • Viewer: Can view all settings and data on an account, but can’t edit or share anything
  • None: This is a role, but without any abilities, perhaps used for someone you intend to add in the future





Reference

  • https://themeisle.com/blog/how-to-add-user-to-google-analytics/

title: Giving access in Google Search Console
description:
To give access to your Google Search account, you can follow these steps:


Step 1

  • Sign in to your Google Search Console account


Step 2

  • Once logged in, you will see a list of the websites within your account on the upper left-hand side. 
  • On the left menu, scroll to the bottom and click on Settings


Step 3

  • Under General settings, click Users and Permissions, you will now see the list of users
  • In the upper right-hand corner, click the blue “Add Users” button, a pop-up will appear



 

Step 4

  • Enter the email address and set permission
  • Click 'Add'




Reference 

  • https://support.google.com/webmasters/answer/7687615?hl=en
  • https://linchpinseo.com/how-to-give-access-to-google-analytics-and-google-search-console/

title: Delegate Access in GoDaddy for a Domain
description:
To delegate access to your GoDaddy account, you can follow these steps


Step 1

  • Go to your GoDaddy Delegate Access page. You might be prompted to sign in


Step 2

  • In the People who can access my account section, select Invite to Access



Step 3

  • Enter the Name and Email address for the person you're inviting




Step 4

  • Select one of the access levels.  For more information on access levels, please read this reference



Step 5

  • Select Invite. An email invitation to access your account will be sent. Once the person accepts, you'll receive notification from GoDaddy




Reference

  • https://in.godaddy.com/help/invite-a-delegate-to-access-my-godaddy-account-12376

title: WordPress Migration file export
description:
To create an export file with all of your site’s content, follow these steps


Step 1

  • Sign in to your WordPress account. Go to Tools → Export

Step 2

  • Download your export file by,
    • Clicking the Export All button 
    • Selecting all content and clicking the Download Export File button


Step 3

  • Wait for the export to process. Once it’s ready, click the Download button that appears


Step 4

  • You will receive a .zip file to save to your computer with the .xml file inside of it
  • Share the .zip file over email


Reference

  • https://wordpress.com/support/export/

title: How Pages section of the dashboard works?
description:
Pages section can be used to create/edit/delete static pages like contact us, about us, etc. This section is available on the left side menu in the PubLive Dashboard as shown below




To create a new static page


Step 1

  • Click + button to create a new static page



Step 2


Enter the Page name, meta title (page name), meta description (brief description), and Content (content for the page), and click Save




Changes will be saved and the respective page will be created and available on the website.


To delete the existing page


Click Delete to delete any existing page

title: How to add members into the dashboard?
description:
The team section in the dashboard will help to provide, update access to team members. 


Adding a new member to the dashboard 


Step 1: In the dashboard, click the Team section from the left menu 



Step 2: Click Add New 



Step 3: Provide email, name, slug (name of the member), role which are mandatory; description, and social profile links not madatory but recommended. Then, click Add New User



After the above step, members will be added to the team section and credentials are sent as an auto-triggered email 


Deleting a member


Step 1: 


Click the bin icon under Action to delete the member from the dashboard



Step 2: 


Click Ok to confirm the delete





This will delete the member from the dashboard permanently


Edit the member roles


Step 1: 


Click the pencil icon to edit the members in the dashboard



Step 2: 


Select the role from the dropdown
















Click Edit User as shown above to update the details of the existing member


Note: Following are the roles and access rights 

  • Owner - can add, delete, and update details of all member roles  + can add, delete and update categories+tags
  • Admin - can add, delete and update subsequent roles like editor, content writer, contributor + can add, delete and update categories+tags
  • Editor - can update and delete content writer, contributor + can add, delete and update categories+tags
  • Content Writer - cannot directly publish content, can send for approval
  • Contributor - member cannot access the dashboard. The owner/admin can handle this member account



Role filters

 

Role filters can show the members of respective roles as shown below

title: How Navbar Configuration works?
description:
The new feature Navbar Configuration is now available in the configuration section of the dashboard.


This feature helps the publishers to create/update/arrange/remove menu items on the website navbar. These customizations can be done by the publishers directly 


Note: This configuration section is accessible only to Owners and Admins




To get started with the Navbar Configuration, go to Navigation -> Click Add tab

On click of Add tab, fill in the following details


  • Tab name - the name of the menu item 
  • Link (optional)  - link of the menu item, can add internal/external links.   
  • Select Parent Tab (optional)- selecting any existing parent item/ menu item from this dropdown, will allow the new menu item to be added as a child item to it  
  • Open in New Tab (optional) 
    • If enabled - opens in new tab 
    • If disabled - opens in same tab   






Note:  Click Save for the changes to reflect in website


1. Dashboard | Menu item as a Link and As Parent Item


Website | Menu item as a Link and As Parent Item





2. Dashboard | Menu item as a Link and As Child Item






Website | Menu item as a Link and As Child Item



Once all the menu items are added, click Save for the changes to be visible on website



3. Dashboard | As a sub-menu item added to a child item


You can also add sub-menu item under a child item as shown below. Example,

  • Home -Parent item
  • Publive - Child category to "Home" parent item
  • Blog website - Sub-category to "Publive" child item




Website | As a sub-menu item added to a child item






Note: Collapsing/expanding options for dropdown menu items, re-ordering of sub-menu items, deleting, editing menu items are possible

title: Adding Static Pages to the website using Custom Format
description:
Now to create any static/custom page, you can utilize the Custom Page format in the dashboard. This allows publishers to create any page, edit URL and use it across in Footer, Navbar, or in any pages for better user navigation and brand positioning 

To get started, 


Step 1: Go to Create -> Click Custom page format



Step 2: Enter all the required information for the static page. Similar to sample shown below





Step 3: For Primary category, please create a new category as follows
 
  • Name: "Pages" 
  • Slug: "publive_pages" and use the same while creating a custom page as shown below

 

Step 4: Preview and Publish the static page

Step 5: Once published, it won't be visible on the website as it is a custom page. You can use the link of this static page in footer or wherever as per your requirement.


Step 6: You can edit the permalink for this custom page anytime using the "Edit Permalink" option as shown below



Link to know more about Permalink Editing - https://publive.freshdesk.com/a/solutions/articles/89000017557

title: Manage all redirection using Redirect Manager in dashboard
description:
You can now manage all post/page link redirection using Redirect Manager in the dashboard to redirect site visitors and search engines from an old URL to a new URL. All redirection will be handled automatically


To get started,


Step 1: Go to the Configuration section -> Redirects

 




Note: Configuration section is visible/accessible to Owners and Admins only



Step 2: All the redirected URLs will be listed here with the following information as shown below 


  • Type - Permanently redirect
  • Old URL
  • New URL
  • Status - Enabled/Disabled
  • Actions - Edit/Delete




Step 2: To add any new redirection, click Add Redirect on the top right and fill the details 

  • Select Redirect Type - Permanently Redirect 
  • Old URL - provide the existing URL
  • New URL - provide the new URL for the same to which the redirection to happen

Click Save once done providing all the details 




  


To edit URL of any article, "Permalink Editing" can be utilized from published section. Link to the help doc to know more - https://publive.freshdesk.com/a/solutions/articles/89000017557

title: How to configure Banner and Announcement for website
description:
We are thrilled to announce that the updated version of Banner and Announcement is now live on the dashboard. To configure it, navigate to the Configuration section and select Banner and Announcement, as shown below.


This feature allows publishers to update, arrange, and remove banners and announcements on website pages directly. 


Note: Only Owners and Admins can access this configuration section.


How to Set Up Banner and Announcement


To set up Banner and Announcement, go to the sidebar and click on the Configuration section.




To get started with Banner and Announcement Configuration, go to the Banner and Announcement section.



Here we have certain fields which are Position, Banner Slot Name, Status, Slot type, Updated on, Updated by and Actions.


  • Position: It shows the slot position, which can be used to visualise using a slot image.


Note: Position has a standard naming convention for slots, which defines the position. The slot name consists of two words: the first word indicates the device, and the second word indicates the page. "HP" stands for Home Page, "CP" stands for Category Page, and "AP" stands for Article Page. The last word indicates the position.

For example, "Mobile_ap_top" indicates that this slot is for the mobile version

on the article page in the top position. as shown in slot icon.


  • Banner Slot Name: It describes slot names, as we can have multiple slots in a single slot position so it helps to differentiate.

  • Status: It shows if the slot is activated or inactivated, we can change the status of the slot using the toggle button.

  • Updated on: It shows the last updated date.

  • Updated by: It shows details of the User who made updates recently.

  • Actions: Using this section you can edit the slot.

  • Slot type: it shows the slot type applied, we currently have 3 different types of slots which are Image type, Rich Text Snippet and Custom  Code Snippet.



Slot Types


Image Type: You can upload an image of different sizes with a URL. To upload an image, follow the media upload feature. Enter the target URL and select the size according to the device. The slot status is active by default. If you don’t want it to be live immediately, you can change the status to inactive. Overrides on category and article pages are also possible.





Rich Text Snippet: Use this for notifications. Enter the notification content in the content field. The slot status is active by default. If you don’t want it to be live immediately, you can change the status to inactive. Overrides on category and article pages are also possible.




Custom Code Snippet: Paste any code (e.g., GAM tag or other scripts) into the head or body. Enter the code in the content field. The slot status is active by default. If you don’t want it to be live immediately, you can change the status to inactive. Overrides on category and article pages are also possible.




Override Functionality



To set an override, click on "Add Override."




Overrides only affect the selected slot type. They can be based on categories, tags, or authors, providing flexibility in content management. You can select at least one of these criteria to customize the override settings. For example, if you choose categories, the override will apply specifically to the content within those categories. Similarly, if you select tags or authors, the override will be tailored to the specified tags or authors. This allows for precise control over the content displayed in the selected slot.


Category Override: If you select a category, the override will apply to that category only. Other articles will remain the same.


Tags & Author Override: If you select tags, the override will apply to any article associated with those tags or authors.


Once you upload an image or fill in the required fields, you can save it, and it will be reflected on the website.


Note: In starting, this section is blank. You need to raise a ticket to activate the required slots before configuring the banner and announcement section.

title: Zapier Integration
description:
Building Webhooks for Publive: Empowering Publisher Integration

Publive introduces powerful webhooks designed to streamline interactions and enhance operational efficiency for publishers. These webhooks enable seamless integration with external systems, allowing publishers to stay informed and take immediate action on critical events within Publive.


Overview

Webhooks in Publive function by triggering HTTP POST requests to user-defined URLs, providing real-time notifications whenever specified events occur. These events range from content updates to member activities, ensuring that publishers can promptly respond to changes and updates in their ecosystem.


Setting up a Webhook

Setting up a webhook in Publive is straightforward and can be managed directly through the Publive Configuration user interface. Key components include:

  • Target URL: This is the endpoint where Publive will send POST requests. It must be a publicly accessible URL.

  • Events: Publishers can subscribe to various events to trigger webhook notifications. Examples include content updates, member activities, and more.

Upon triggering an event, Publive sends a JSON payload to the configured endpoint, containing detailed information about the event. This data can facilitate actions ranging from simple notifications to complex system integrations.


Available Events

Publive supports a comprehensive range of events that publishers can subscribe to via webhooks:

  • Content Management: Events related to posts, tags, categories, and site changes.

  • Member Activities: Events such as member additions, edits, and deletions.

  • Reader Engagement: Includes activities like newsletter subscriptions, downloads, and interactions with paywall content.

  • User-Generated Content (UGC): Notifications for submissions, approvals, and publication of user-generated content.

  • Navigation Updates: Updates to website navigation elements like navbar and footer.

  • Notifications: Events related to push notifications and newsletter campaigns.


EventDescription
site.changedTriggered whenever any content changes in your site data or settings
post.addedTriggered whenever a post is added to Publive
post.deletedTriggered whenever a post is deleted from Publive
post.editedTriggered whenever a post is edited in Publive
post.publishedTriggered whenever a post is published to Publive
post.published.editedTriggered whenever a published post is edited in Publive
post.unpublishedTriggered whenever a post is unpublished from Publive
post.scheduledTriggered whenever a post is scheduled to be published in Publive
post.unscheduledTriggered whenever a post is unscheduled from publishing in Publive
post.rescheduledTriggered whenever a post is rescheduled to publish in Publive
tag.addedTriggered whenever a tag is added to Publive
tag.editedTriggered whenever a tag is edited in Publive
tag.deletedTriggered whenever a tag is deleted from Publive
member.addedTriggered whenever a member is added to Publive
member.editedTriggered whenever a member is edited in Publive
member.deletedTriggered whenever a member is deleted from Publive
reader.added  newsletter.subscribeTriggered whenever a reader signup and also triggered newsletter subscribe
reader.downloadedTriggered whenever reader data downloaded
newsletter.subscribeTriggered whenever a reader subscribe newsletter
newsletter.unsubscribeTriggered whenever a reader unsubscribe newsletter
newsletter.sentTriggered whenever newsletter campaign sent
category.addedTriggered whenever a category added to Publive
category.editedTriggered whenever a category edited in Publive
category.deletedTriggered whenever a category is deleted from Publive
paywall.subscription.subscribeTriggered whenever a reader subscribe for paywall content on publive
paywall.subscription.expiredTriggered whenever reader subscription expired
paywall.subscription.renewedTriggered whenever reader subscription renewed
ugc.post.submissionTriggered whenever a ugc submission received
ugc.post.seenTriggered whenever a ugc submission seen
ugc.post.approvedTriggered whenever a ugc submission approved
ugc.post.publishedTriggered whenever a ugc submission published
navigation.navbar.editedTriggered whenever an edit occur in navbar
navigation.footer.editedTriggered whenever an edit occur in footer data
pushnotification.sentTriggered whenever an push notification sent on publive



Integration with Zapier

Publive web hooks seamlessly integrate with Zapier, empowering publishers to automate workflows and connect Publive events with over thousands of other apps. For instance, when a new article is published (post.published), Zapier can automatically trigger actions based on predefined rules, such as sending notifications or updating external databases.


Sample Prefill Data

When a post is published (post.published), the webhook payload includes essential metadata about the article, such as:

post.published:
           
{
"title": "testing batching for newsletter locks",
"legacy_url": "/ignore/testing-batching-for-newsletter-locks-2739",
"banner_url": "https://img-cdn.thepublive.com/demo/media/media_files/CphhvD4a3E1B736yCjXy.png",
"type": "Article",
"status": "Published",
"absolute_url": "https://demo.lvh.me:8081/ignore/testing-batching-for-newsletter-locks-2739"
}


This data enables integrations to immediately act upon published content, enhancing publisher productivity and engagement.

Publive’s robust webhook system empowers publishers with real-time insights and operational efficiency, ensuring seamless integration with external systems and enabling swift action on critical events. By leveraging Publive webhooks, publishers can automate workflows, enhance user engagement, and stay ahead in a dynamic digital publishing landscape.

title: How to add guest contributor as Byline ?
description:
The Team section in the dashboard allows admins to add, update, and manage team members by providing and modifying their access rights.


Follow the steps to create new contributor in team section

  • Step 1: Login to the Publive Dashboard.


  • Step 2: Navigate to the Team or Add Team Members


  • Step 3: Click "Add Team member" to open the Create Member page




  • Step 4:Fill in the required details:

    • Email (Mandatory)
    • Name (Mandatory)
    • Slug (Mandatory – enter the team member's unique identifier)
    • Role (Mandatory – select the Contributor role)
    • Meta Title & Meta Description (Optional but recommended)
    • Author Display Order (Default: 1000) 
    • Description (optional)
    • Hide Author on Website (if do not want to show the name on the website turn off the button)
    • Add Social Profiles (optional)



  • Step 5:Click "Save" to add the team member.



Step 6: Deleting a Member

  •  Click the delete icon under the actions column.


  • Click "Delete" to confirm the deletion but Deletion is not possible when  this member has posts that exist.


  • The member will be permanently removed from the dashboard.

Step 7: Editing a Member’s Role

  • Click the edit icon  under the actions column.


  • Select a new role from the drop down menu and Click "Save" to update the details.



Note:A Contributor cannot access the dashboard directly.The owner/admin manages the contributor's account.



Role filters :Role filters can show the members of respective roles as shown below :


Link the Contributor to the Byline:

  • Go to the Content Type section.
  • Navigate to Credits and search for the contributor’s email ID.


  • Add the contributor to the credits to ensure their name appears as a byline on the website.



    Conclusion:

    Adding a Guest Contributor as a Byline helps digital publishers maintain content diversity and credibility by featuring industry experts, freelance writers, and guest authors. It enhances audience engagement, builds authority, and allows for fresh perspectives without granting full dashboard access. By properly attributing contributors through Team Management and Credits, publishers can ensure transparency, streamline editorial workflows, and optimize content visibility on their platforms.

title: Advanced SEO Modules : SEO Meta Configurations
description:
The SEO meta patterns section provides guidance on following best practices for creating meta tags, including title tags and meta descriptions. You can use the codes we have provided or create your own as per your requirements. These patterns are designed to enhance a website’s search engine optimization by improving search engine rankings, increasing click-through rates (CTR), and boosting overall user engagement.


Where to Find the Feature

The Advanced SEO Module feature is seamlessly integrated into the Configuration Section of the Dashboard.


Feature Availability

  • The Advanced SEO Module feature is available to Owner and Admins users across all accounts.
  • This feature is available for plans Advanced and higher


Getting Started with the Feature


Step 1: Login in to Dashboard


Step 2: Go to Configurations, inside Advanced SEO Modules choose Misc.


Step 3: Inside Misc., go to SEO Meta


Step 4: Apply fallback meta patterns to all category pages, tag pages and author pages for uniform metadata across categories and Save it.

*Note : The homepage is already optimized, and for all posts, publishers can set up meta values at the post level.

meta patterns for all category pages



For "All Category" Title Patterns you may set the pattern as following

{{category.name}} |  {{publisher.name}}


For "All Category" Description Patterns you may set the pattern as following

Explore the latest in {{category.name}} on {{publisher.name}}. Stay informed with top stories and in-depth coverage.


meta patterns for all tag pages


For "All Tags" Title Patterns you may set the pattern as following

{{tag.name}} |  {{publisher.name}}


For "All Tags" Description Patterns you may set the pattern as following

Explore the latest in {{tag.name}} on {{publisher.name}}. Stay informed with top stories and in-depth coverage.


meta patterns for all author pages


For "All Author" Title Patterns you may set the pattern as following

{{author.name}} |  {{publisher.name}}


For "All Author" Description Patterns you may set the pattern as following

Explore the latest in {{author.name}} on {{publisher.name}}. Stay informed with top stories and in-depth coverage.


Benefits for Digital Publishers

  • Improved Search Engine Visibility: Well-optimized meta tags (title, and description) increase the chances of ranking higher in search results, driving more organic traffic.
  • Increased Click-Through Rates (CTR): Compelling meta titles and descriptions encourage users to click on your links by making your content stand out among search results.
  • Efficient Content Indexing by Search Engines: Meta tags (e.g., canonical tags, robots meta tags) help search engines understand the structure of your website and avoid issues like duplicate content.


For any further assistance create a ticket using help widget available in the dashboard or reach to support@thepublive.com

Efficient Content Indexing by Search Engines: Meta tags (e.g., canonical tags, robots meta tags) help search engines understand the structure of your website and avoid issues like duplicate content.
